# React Migration Phase 0 Inventory

This inventory is the working guardrail for the native React, Tailwind, and Base UI migration described in `docs/spark/2026-06-08-react-runtime-migration-design.md`.

## Baseline

- Working tree baseline was clean before Phase 0 edits.
- `npm run typecheck` initially failed on existing Base UI wrapper type mismatches and an AI model picker island naming mismatch.
- `npm run typecheck` now passes after wrapper and compatibility island fixes.
- `npm run build` passes.
- The earlier missing `scripts/run-tests.mjs` and `scripts/smoke-extension.mjs` entries have been restored as of the 2026-06-21 reproducible verification cleanup.
- `npm test` now runs the repository `scripts/run-tests.mjs` entry and passes by executing `npm run typecheck` followed by `npm run build`.
- `npm run smoke:extension` now runs the repository `scripts/smoke-extension.mjs` entry and passes against the current built unpacked extension.

## Base UI Documentation Read

The migration requirement to read Base UI docs before implementation has been satisfied for this Phase 0 baseline work:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`
- `https://base-ui.com/react/components/select`
- `https://base-ui.com/react/components/drawer`
- `https://base-ui.com/react/components/progress`
- `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog.md`
- `https://base-ui.com/react/components/collapsible`
- `https://base-ui.com/react/handbook/composition`
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/toolbar.md`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/input.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/progress.md` were read before the popup smart classifier Tailwind migration. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Button/Toolbar/Input/Progress wrappers with explicit button types and React-owned state/event handlers while moving visual styling into Tailwind constants.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the popup ref cleanup and smart overlay shell slices. It confirms Base UI is a composable, styling-agnostic React component and utility library, and that Tailwind examples are written for Tailwind CSS v4. The project currently uses Tailwind CSS v4.3.0, so examples generally align with the installed version but still need adaptation to local tokens and extension constraints.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab background media layer slice. That slice does not touch a specific Base UI primitive, so no component-specific Base UI page was required.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab bookmark menu, background/drag layer, background mask shell, and wallpaper loading indicator Tailwind migration slices. The bookmark menu slice keeps the existing Base UI Button/Input/Select/InlineMenu composition; the background, mask, and loading indicator slices do not touch a specific Base UI primitive API.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button` were re-read before the newtab state-panel Tailwind migration. The slice keeps existing Base UI Button wrappers with explicit `type="button"` semantics and moves only the state shell/text/loader/action layout into component-owned Tailwind classes.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button` were re-read before the newtab onboarding strip Tailwind migration. The slice keeps existing Base UI Button wrappers with explicit `type="button"` semantics and moves the onboarding surface/copy/action styling into component-owned Tailwind classes.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the options route/nav ownership slice. The Base UI Collapsible documentation was also read because the slice keeps the options availability nav group as a controlled `Collapsible.Root` with `Trigger` and `Panel`.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/collapsible` were re-read before the options nav sublist selector retirement. The docs confirm Base UI remains styling-agnostic, the availability nav group can stay controlled with `open`/`onOpenChange`, and `Collapsible.Panel` accepts `className`, `keepMounted`, hidden state, and open/closed transition attributes.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/collapsible` were re-read before the options nav group data-marker retirement. The slice keeps the same controlled Base UI Collapsible `Root`/`Trigger`/`Panel` composition, relies on Base UI state/ARIA attributes plus the stable `aria-controls`/panel id relation, and removes only app-local debug/compatibility data markers.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/collapsible` were re-read before the options nav group trigger class retirement. The slice keeps the documented Collapsible `Trigger` as a native button with `className` styling, preserves the controlled `Root`/`Panel` relationship, and removes only the obsolete app-local class token.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/collapsible` were re-read before the options navigation active class retirement. The slice keeps the existing controlled Base UI Collapsible `Root`/`Trigger`/`Panel` composition and moves only local navigation selected-state styling from a rendered class hook to the existing `aria-current="page"` semantic marker.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md` were re-read before the dashboard folder tree active-state class retirement. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Button wrapper composition with explicit `type="button"` semantics and moves only folder tree selected-state styling from conditional rendered class constants to the existing `aria-selected="true"` semantic marker.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md` were re-read before the dashboard folder drop target state class retirement. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Button wrapper composition with explicit `type="button"` semantics and moves only folder drop target active/moving styling from conditional rendered class constants to the existing `aria-selected="true"` state plus a React-owned `data-dashboard-moving="true"` marker.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/input.md` were re-read before the 2026-06-09 options direct-render cleanup slices. Those slices continue to use the existing Base UI Button/Input wrappers and did not introduce new primitives.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/input`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/checkbox`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/menu`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover` were re-read before the dashboard direct-render naming/docs cleanup. The source change did not alter primitive composition, but the dashboard view currently uses these existing app wrappers.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/input` were re-read before the availability controls direct-render slice. The slice uses the existing Base UI Popover/Button/Input wrappers in controlled React state.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button` were re-read before the dashboard drag hover/moving marker retirement. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Button wrapper composition for folder drop targets with explicit `type="button"`, `role="option"`, and `aria-selected`, and changes only React-owned visual state class application.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog.md` were re-read before the availability overview validation and Base UI inert cleanup. The fix keeps dashboard Popover and managed Dialog composition on the documented primitives while preventing hidden/closed layers from applying active page inertness.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button` were re-read before the availability/history scope trigger slice. The slice uses the existing Base UI Button wrapper with explicit `type="button"` and `focusableWhenDisabled` for disabled scope-change triggers.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/input` were re-read before the folder picker roving focus cleanup. The slice keeps the Base UI Button/Input wrappers while moving focus ownership from controller DOM queries to React refs.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog.md` were re-read before the managed modal open/focus cleanup. Local Base UI Dialog type definitions were also checked to confirm `Dialog.Popup` supports function-valued `initialFocus` and `finalFocus`.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/input.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/menu.md` were re-read before the dashboard typed-event cleanup. Local Base UI Popover and Menu type definitions were also checked for `nativeButton`, `onOpenChange`, and controlled trigger behavior.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog.md` were re-read before the newtab dashboard overlay node refs cleanup. The slice keeps the existing controlled Base UI Dialog composition while moving dashboard trigger/overlay/frame node ownership into React-published refs.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/checkbox.md` were re-read before the dashboard delegated-control cleanup. The slice keeps Base UI Button/Checkbox composition while moving React-rendered controls to typed events.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/menu.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md` were re-read before the dashboard tag-editor close focus return cleanup. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Menu/Button composition and moves the close-time focus target into React refs/effects.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover.md` were re-read before the dashboard tag-editor panel visibility/position cleanup. Local Base UI Popover type definitions were checked for the installed Positioner and Popup API surface.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the dashboard drag overlay visual-state cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it kept existing React rendering and moved overlay classes, ARIA, hover state, and preview transform into dashboard view state.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the dashboard results virtualization/favicons cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved result grid classes, favicon load/error events, and favicon fallback rendering further into React-owned state.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover.md` were re-read before the dashboard popover anchor cleanup. The slice keeps Base UI Popover composition and replaces document-query anchors with React refs.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover.md`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/input.md` were re-read before the dashboard results observer/listener ownership slice. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Popover/Button/Input composition while splitting the dashboard panel into smaller React components and moving result-grid scroll/resize listener ownership into React refs/effects.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/menu.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover.md` were re-read before the dashboard panel keydown/pointerdown event ownership slice. The slice kept the existing Base UI Button/Menu/Popover composition while first moving dashboard root event listener ownership into the React panel lifecycle; the later document-event cleanup removed that temporary custom dashboard panel event bridge.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the dashboard virtual metrics snapshot slice. The slice did not touch a new Base UI primitive API; it moved result-container measurement data further into React-owned event payloads.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the dashboard stable-height/list-activity cleanup. The slice did not touch a new Base UI primitive API; it narrowed remaining result-DOM reads by using React-owned metrics and dashboard entry lifecycle state first.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the dashboard results scroll request cleanup. The slice did not touch a new Base UI primitive API; it moved programmatic result scrolling into a React-owned ref/effect command path.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the dashboard drag hit-detection cleanup. The slice did not touch a new Base UI primitive API; it moved drag/drop target hover detection from controller global DOM hit-testing into React-owned overlay and folder-card pointer events.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the options DOM cache removal cleanup. The slice did not touch a new Base UI primitive API; it deleted the now-unused legacy options DOM ref cache.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab icon layout store cleanup. The slice did not change Base UI primitive APIs; it moved icon layout CSS variables and page vertical-center attributes from controller DOM writes into React-owned content/page stores while keeping the existing Base UI settings controls.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab search layout store cleanup. The slice did not change Base UI primitive APIs; it moved search slot CSS variables, auto-center attributes, and page collision offset writes into React-owned search/content view state while keeping the existing Base UI settings controls.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab drag active-class cleanup. The slice did not change Base UI primitive APIs; it moved active dragged card/tile/folder `dragging` classes from controller `classList` writes into React-owned view state while preserving the existing drag ghost compatibility layer.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab drag ghost React layer cleanup. The slice did not change Base UI primitive APIs; it moved Speed Dial, bookmark tile, and folder drag ghosts from controller-created DOM clones into the React-owned drag layer store/host.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab drag preview transform cleanup. The slice did not change Base UI primitive APIs; it moved Speed Dial and bookmark reorder preview transform/z-index updates from direct DOM style writes into React-owned card/tile view-model style state.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/drawer`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/tabs`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog` were re-read before the newtab settings drawer inert and featured picker content-state cleanup. The drawer fix follows the documented non-modal Drawer pattern with `modal={false}` and `disablePointerDismissal`, while the tabs and dialog composition remains on the documented controlled primitives.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md` were re-read before the newtab featured picker initial-focus cleanup. The slice keeps the controlled Base UI Dialog composition, uses the documented Button composition for the focusable wallpaper card surface, and moves initial focus to React refs/effects.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab featured picker preview hydration cleanup. The slice did not introduce a new Base UI primitive; it kept the existing controlled Dialog and documented Button composition while moving card preview image hydration/fallback and hover-preview data ownership into React state/refs.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/input` were re-read before the newtab source folder candidate focus cleanup. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Button/Input wrappers while moving candidate-list focus execution from controller DOM queries into React refs/effects.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/drawer.md`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/tabs.md`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/input.md` were re-read before the newtab settings drawer focus/scroll request cleanup. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Drawer/Tabs/Button/Input composition while moving settings drawer section focus and scroll execution into React refs/effects.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/drawer.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/tabs.md` were re-read before the newtab settings drawer Tab focus trap cleanup. That earlier slice kept the controlled non-modal Base UI Drawer and controlled Tabs composition while moving the drawer Tab wrap listener into the React drawer component lifecycle.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog.md` were re-read before the newtab dashboard overlay Tab focus trap cleanup. That earlier slice kept the existing controlled Base UI Dialog composition while moving the dashboard overlay Tab wrap listener into the React overlay component lifecycle.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/drawer.md` were re-read before the newtab component focus-trap removal cleanup. The slice removes the remaining component-scoped hand-written Tab wrap listeners from the dashboard overlay, featured background dialog, and settings drawer, and lets documented Base UI Dialog/Drawer popup focus management own Tab containment. The settings drawer now uses Base UI Drawer `modal="trap-focus"` so focus is trapped without scroll locking or pointer inerting.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog.md` were re-read before the featured background modal final-focus follow-up. Installed Base UI Dialog type definitions were also checked for function-valued `finalFocus` and `modal="trap-focus"` semantics.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab instant wallpaper state host cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved post-mount instant wallpaper `<html>` class, dataset, and CSS variable updates from controller helpers into a React-owned host while preserving the pre-React startup script.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab bookmark menu positioning cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved viewport-clamped bookmark menu `left`/`top` updates from imperative element style writes into React-owned state.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button` were re-read before the dashboard natural-search state marker retirement. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Button wrapper usage with explicit `type="button"`, `aria-pressed`, accessible labels, and typed `toggle-natural-search` dispatch; only the local rendered state marker was replaced by React-owned Tailwind class constants.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/tooltip.md` were re-read before the dashboard icon tooltip marker retirement. The slice uses Base UI Tooltip for hover/focus hints while preserving button/link accessible names through `aria-label`.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/menu` were re-read before the dashboard action menu root marker retirement. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Menu/InlineMenu composition and uses the documented trigger/popup semantics as the document-event containment boundary.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover` were re-read before the dashboard tag popover marker retirement. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Popover composition and uses the trigger `aria-controls` plus popup `id` relation as the containment boundary.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the options tag cloud physics event-state cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved tag word discovery, dragging class state, and click suppression out of DOM `querySelector`/`classList`/`dataset` paths while retaining imperative transform writes only for the high-frequency animation loop.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the shared motion dead-code cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it removed unused exit-motion helpers that still mutated DOM class and dataset state.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the shared DOM helper deletion. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it deleted the unused `src/shared/dom.ts` helper after all old runtime consumers had been removed.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the popup CSS token/reset consolidation. The slice only collapsed popup page token/reset scaffolding and did not change a specific Base UI primitive API, so no component-specific Base UI page was required.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab search layout measurement refs cleanup. The slice did not touch a Base UI primitive API; it moved content layout node discovery from controller selectors into React-owned refs while preserving necessary DOM rect measurement.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab featured modal node refs cleanup. The slice did not alter Base UI primitive composition; it moved featured gallery modal/grid node discovery from controller `cachedEl` lookups into React-owned refs published by the controlled Dialog component.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was checked for the dashboard external-store flush cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it removed the last dashboard `react-dom` `flushSync` compatibility publish path after dashboard focus, scroll, metrics, and tag-editor positioning had moved into React refs/effects.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/drawer.md` were re-read before the newtab settings trigger / featured picker return-focus cleanup. Installed Base UI Dialog and Drawer type definitions were also checked for function-valued `finalFocus`.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/drawer.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md` were re-read before the newtab settings drawer/backdrop node refs cleanup. The slice keeps the existing controlled non-modal Base UI Drawer composition, uses the existing Button wrapper for the React-owned backdrop button, and moves settings drawer/backdrop node discovery into React-published refs.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/drawer`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/switch`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/select`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/handbook/composition` were re-read before the newtab settings drawer focus-ref cleanup. The Drawer docs confirm `modal="trap-focus"`, `initialFocus`, and `finalFocus`; the Switch and Select docs separate hidden input refs from the focusable root/trigger controls; and the Composition guide confirms composed controls must forward refs.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab menu motion-spring alias retirement. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it only moved a React-owned Tailwind animation utility from a newtab-local motion alias to the shared `--ui-ease-spring` token.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab menu/search motion-fast alias retirement. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it only moved React-owned Tailwind animation/duration utilities from a newtab-local motion alias to the shared `--ui-motion-fast` token.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab shell node/event binding cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved `#newtab-root` node ownership from controller `cachedEl` lookup into a React-published shell ref and kept the existing React-rendered bookmark menu components.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab source navigation refs cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved source-section scrolling/focus from controller `cachedEl(anchorId)` into React-owned folder section refs and kept navigation controls as React Button components.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab Speed Dial node refs cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved Speed Dial grid/card discovery from controller selectors into React-published refs while preserving existing React-rendered link cards.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button` were re-read before the newtab bookmark/folder drag node refs cleanup. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Button-backed folder header with `type="button"` while moving bookmark tile, tile icon, folder section, folder header, and grid discovery from controller selectors into React-published refs.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button` were re-read before the newtab folder section shell Tailwind migration. The slice did not change Base UI primitive APIs; it moved the static folder section shell visuals into React-owned Tailwind classes while preserving existing Button-backed folder header controls with explicit `type="button"` semantics.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab bookmark reorder status Tailwind migration. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved the React-rendered status paragraph visuals into colocated Tailwind classes while preserving the existing `role="status"`, marker class, and `data-tone` hook.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab quick-access portal Tailwind migration. - `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab bookmark tile/icon and Speed Dial Tailwind migrations. These slices did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; they moved native anchor/span/section visuals into colocated Tailwind class helpers while preserving stable marker/data hooks and React-owned drag-layer behavior.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button` were re-read before the newtab React-owned drag-start cleanup. The slice keeps Speed Dial and bookmark cards as native anchors, keeps folder headers as Base UI Button-backed `type="button"` controls, and moves drag-start pointerdown ownership from shell delegation into React component handlers.

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab React-owned click-suppression cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved drag-release click suppression into React-owned click handlers.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab React-owned blank context-menu cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved the shell blank-area add-bookmark context-menu entry from a controller-owned native listener into the React shell `onContextMenu`.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab React-owned search context-menu boundary cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved the search-area right-click exemption from controller DOM `closest()` checks into the React search shell `onContextMenu`.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/menu` were re-read before the newtab bookmark menu action-hook retirement. The docs confirm Base UI Menu remains composable/styling-agnostic, uses `Menu.Root`/`Portal`/`Positioner`/`Popup`/`Item` anatomy, supports controlled `open`/`onOpenChange`, `closeOnClick`, and `nativeButton`; the slice kept the existing inline Menu composition and added only a React-owned menu-surface Escape close request around it.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog` were re-read before the newtab featured modal backdrop/close ownership cleanup. The Dialog docs confirm controlled `open`/`onOpenChange`, `Dialog.Close`, and `disablePointerDismissal` behavior; the slice keeps the existing controlled Dialog composition while moving backdrop close detection into React overlay events.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab bookmark-menu outside pointer cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved outside pointer dismissal from a controller-owned `document.addEventListener('pointerdown', ...)` listener into the React app shell `onPointerDownCapture`.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab document keyboard ownership cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved the top-level document keydown listener installation into the React app lifecycle while keeping keyboard business actions in the controller.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab window resize ownership cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved the top-level window resize listener installation into the React app lifecycle while keeping resize business actions in the controller.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the newtab window pointer ownership cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved top-level `pointermove`/`pointerup`/`pointercancel` listener installation into the React app lifecycle while keeping drag business actions in the controller.

The slice keeps the existing Base UI Switch and Collapsible composition and changes only the React handler path from a window custom event to a direct controller action.
- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/switch`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/tooltip` were re-read before the options feature settings event retirement. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Switch and Tooltip composition and changes only the React handler path from a window custom event to a direct controller action; the tooltip trigger retains its accessible name.

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before the options dashboard-fullscreen chrome native-hidden cleanup. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it moved fullscreen chrome hiding from Tailwind ancestor hidden utilities to React-owned native `hidden` props.

Local package type definitions under `node_modules/@base-ui/react` were also checked for the exact installed API surface:

- `Select.Root` supports `modal`.
- `Select.Portal` supports `container`.
- `Drawer.Popup` supports `initialFocus` and `finalFocus`.
- `Progress.Indicator` is a Base UI div primitive and supports standard component props such as class names, data attributes, and style.
- `Popover.Trigger` and `Menu.Item` support `nativeButton`; dashboard now sets that explicitly when rendering native `button` elements through Base UI composition.
- `Dialog.Popup` supports `initialFocus`, `finalFocus`, `aria-describedby`, data transition attributes, and the documented `Root > Trigger > Portal > Backdrop > Popup > Title` composition.

AI Elements documentation read for the model selector repair:

- `https://elements.ai-sdk.dev/components/model-selector`
- `https://elements.ai-sdk.dev/components/model-selector.md`
- `https://elements.ai-sdk.dev/api/registry/model-selector.json`

The AI Elements model selector is a searchable command-palette pattern with a dialog trigger, hidden dialog title, input, grouped list, empty state, model rows, provider logos from `https://models.dev/logos/{provider}.svg`, and optional shortcut/separator/logo-group/name helpers. The local implementation uses Base UI Dialog plus React-rendered semantic lists instead of adding a new cmdk runtime.

## Current Controller and Runtime Boundaries

The original legacy runtime entry files have already been replaced or removed in the current working tree. The remaining migration work is concentrated in controller-style files and React-owned helper hooks that still bridge imperative browser behavior during extraction.

Removed historical runtime entry points:

- `src/popup/popup-runtime.ts`
- `src/popup/components/PopupRuntimeIslands.tsx`
- `src/newtab/newtab-runtime.ts`
- `src/newtab/components/RuntimeIslands.tsx`
- `src/options/options-runtime.ts`
- `src/options/sections/dashboard-runtime.ts`
- `src/options/sections/tag-cloud-runtime.ts`

| File | Approx. Lines | Current Surface | DOM Operation Count | Migration Role |
| --- | ---: | --- | ---: | --- |
| `src/popup/popup-controller.ts` | 4,209 | `usePopupController`, popup view-model/state actions | 7 | Phase 1 controller extraction target |
| `src/newtab/newtab-controller.ts` | 11,798 | newtab controller, settings, drag, dashboard overlay, background handling | 155 | Phase 2 controller extraction target |
| `src/options/options-controller.ts` | 9,875 | options controller, route handling, management workflows, modal state | 225 | Phase 3 controller extraction target |
| `src/options/sections/dashboard-controller.ts` | 5,988 | dashboard controller helpers, event handlers, render functions | 96 | Phase 3 dashboard extraction target |
| `src/options/components/useTagCloudPhysics.ts` | 636 | React-owned tag cloud physics hook | 9 | Phase 3 helper retained behind component ref/effect |

DOM operation counts were gathered from occurrences of selectors and imperative DOM operations such as `querySelector`, `getElementById`, `addEventListener`, `replaceChildren`, `textContent =`, `classList`, `createElement`, `appendChild`, and direct style mutation.

## Runtime Island Files

All tracked runtime island bridge files have now been removed or converted into normal React component modules.

Deleted historical island bridge files:

- `src/popup/components/PopupRuntimeIslands.tsx`
- `src/newtab/components/RuntimeIslands.tsx`
- `src/options/components/DashboardRuntimeIslands.tsx`

The dashboard replacement is `src/options/components/DashboardView.tsx`, backed by `dashboard-view-store.ts` and `dashboard-view-types.ts`. These files expose React view state and components; they do not create extra React roots or expose island render APIs.

### Options AI Provider Slice

The AI provider settings area in the General options panel has been moved from old DOM placeholders and old model picker islands into React-owned components:

- `CorePanels.tsx` now renders `AiProviderSettings` directly in `GeneralPanel`.
- `ai-provider-settings-store.ts` exposes the controller-published AI provider settings state through `useSyncExternalStore`.
- `options-controller.ts` publishes provider settings state with `publishAiProviderSettings` instead of mounting a separate React root into `#ai-provider-settings-host`.
- `AiProviderSettings.tsx` owns the AI provider card, API Key input, reveal switch, Base URL/API style/batch controls, fetch/test/save buttons, connectivity copy, and the custom model dialog.
- `AiModelSelector.tsx` owns the model selector state bridge and grouped/searchable model list.
- `src/ui/ai/ModelSelector.tsx` now matches the AI Elements model selector structure with Base UI Dialog primitives, searchable input, list/group/item/empty helpers, provider logos, model names, and selected checks.
- The `#general:ai-provider` anchor attention effect now flows through the AI provider React event bridge; `AiProviderSettings` owns the temporary `attention` class instead of `options-controller.ts` mutating it with `classList`.
- `syncAiNamingSettingsDraftFromDom` and `saveAiNamingSettingsFromDom` were renamed to `syncAiNamingSettingsDraftFromState` and `saveAiNamingSettingsFromState` because provider inputs are React state driven.

Deleted legacy AI provider/model picker bridge files:

- `src/options/components/AiModelSelectorIsland.tsx`
- `src/options/components/AiModelTools.tsx`
- `src/options/components/AiModelToolsIsland.ts`
- `src/options/components/AiProviderSelectIsland.tsx`
- `src/options/components/AiProviderSettingsIsland.ts`
- `src/options/components/ai-model-tools-events.ts`
- `src/options/components/ai-model-tools-types.ts`

The old AI model modal, picker modal, picker focus trap, DOM refs, model tools event bridge, provider select island, provider settings host, and direct AI provider input listeners have been removed from `options-controller.ts` and `shared-options/dom.ts`. The controller now receives typed React events for provider settings and model selection.

Validation after this slice:

- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warnings about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import and large chunks.
- `git diff --check` reports only CRLF working copy warnings.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- A Playwright popup smart-classifier smoke with a minimal Chrome extension API and AI response stub confirmed `body` stays free of `smart-active`, `#popup-app-shell` receives `app-shell smart-active`, a smart classifier card renders, and console warnings remain 0.
- Follow-up verification after moving the AI provider attention class into React: `npm run typecheck` passes and `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warnings about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import and large chunks.
- `git diff --check` reports only CRLF working copy warnings.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- Playwright smoke against `http://127.0.0.1:5173/src/options/options.html#general:ai-provider` confirmed the model selector trigger exists, opens a Base UI Dialog popup, focuses the search input, and renders model items.
- A follow-up Playwright smoke of `#general:ai-provider` opened the model selector, focused the search input, rendered 9 model items, and reported 0 console warnings.
- Follow-up verification after removing `AiProviderSettingsIsland.ts`: `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `button.md`, `dialog.md`, `collapsible.md`, and `select.md` were read; `npm run typecheck`, `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff`, `npm run build`, and `git diff --check` all pass with only the existing Vite build warnings and CRLF working copy warnings. A targeted Playwright smoke confirmed `#ai-provider-settings-host` is gone, `#ai-provider-settings` renders directly, API Key/Base URL controlled inputs update, and the model selector popup opens.

#### Options Feature Settings Slice

Completed after the AI provider direct-render follow-up:

- `CorePanels.tsx` now renders `FeatureSettingsControls` directly in `GeneralPanel`.
- `feature-settings-store.ts` exposes the controller-published feature switch state through `useSyncExternalStore`.
- `options-controller.ts` publishes feature switch state with `publishFeatureSettingsControls` instead of mounting a separate React root into `#feature-settings-controls`.
- `FeatureSettingsControls.tsx` subscribes to the store and continues to use the existing Base UI `SwitchControl` wrapper for the four feature switches.
- `shared-options/dom.ts` no longer caches `dom.featureSettingsControls`.
- Deleted `src/options/components/FeatureSettingsControlsIsland.ts`.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/switch.md` were read before implementation.
- Residual searches confirm no `FeatureSettingsControlsIsland`, `renderFeatureSettingsControlsIsland`, `feature-settings-controls`, or `featureSettingsControls` references remain.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- A targeted Playwright feature settings smoke confirmed the legacy host is gone, the four React-owned Base UI switches render, and clicking `自动分析` dispatches the typed `options:feature-settings-change` event.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warnings about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import and large chunks.
- `git diff --check` reports only CRLF working copy warnings.

#### Options Content Snapshot Settings Slice

- `CorePanels.tsx` now renders `ContentSnapshotControls` directly in `GeneralPanel`.
- `content-snapshot-store.ts` exposes the controller-published content snapshot settings state through `useSyncExternalStore`.
- `options-controller.ts` publishes content snapshot state with `publishContentSnapshotControls` instead of mounting a separate React root into `#content-snapshot-controls`.
- `ContentSnapshotControls.tsx` subscribes to the store and continues to use the existing Base UI `SwitchControl` and `Collapsible` wrappers.

#### Options Dashboard Direct Render Slice

Completed after the folder picker results direct-render slice:

- Added `DashboardView.tsx`, `dashboard-view-store.ts`, and `dashboard-view-types.ts`.
- `DashboardPanel.tsx` now renders dashboard title, cards title, search controls/chips, breadcrumbs, status, selection bar, folder tree/count, results, drag overlay content, and tag editor content directly.
- `dashboard-controller.ts` publishes dashboard view models through `publishDashboardViewState` instead of calling `renderDashboard*Island` helpers.
- Dashboard virtual scrolling still computes the active window in the controller, but React renders the current static or virtual card set from view state.
- Existing delegated dashboard actions and `data-dashboard-*` attributes remain as a compatibility bridge while dashboard event ownership continues to move into React.
- `DashboardRuntimeIslands.tsx` was deleted, and the new dashboard view/store/type files were named without `Runtime` to avoid retaining old runtime terminology.
- `dashboard-view-store.ts` originally kept a synchronous `flushSync` publish because the dashboard controller still restored focus, measured layout, and positioned popovers immediately after publishing view state. Later cleanup moved those behaviors behind React refs/effects and removed the `flushSync` bridge.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, the existing Base UI Button/Input/Checkbox/Menu/Popover docs, and the installed type definitions covered the primitives already used by the migrated dashboard view.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- Residual source searches confirm no `DashboardRuntimeIslands`, `renderDashboard*Island`, `createDashboard*Island`, dashboard virtual island node pool helpers, or `data-react-island-host` references remain.
- Remaining dashboard direct DOM access is currently limited to controller-owned compatibility behavior for drag measurement/positioning, virtualization measurement and scroll bookkeeping, rendered-id reconciliation, and synchronous view publishing; these are the next cleanup targets, not island roots.

#### Options Dashboard Typed Event Slice

Completed after the options overview direct-render cleanup:

- Added `dashboard-view-events.ts` and wired `DASHBOARD_VIEW_ACTION_EVENT` through `options-controller.ts` and `dashboard-lazy.ts`.
- Dashboard search query changes, clear search, natural-search toggle, selection bar actions, card menu open/close, card menu actions, tag editor draft changes, tag editor save/cancel, clear-AI, regenerate-AI, search-help toggle, and dashboard exit now dispatch typed React events from `DashboardPanel.tsx`/`DashboardView.tsx`.
- Removed the `data-dashboard-action` compatibility bridge from dashboard React controls and deleted the delegated controller action branch that handled those actions.
- Removed the old `data-dashboard-toggle-menu` click bridge. Base UI `Menu.Root` now reports card menu open state through `InlineMenu.onOpenChange`, and the controller publishes updated card view state from that typed event.
- `dashboard-query` and `dashboard-tag-editor-input` no longer flow through the controller-owned delegated `input` handler; it now only services dashboard selection checkboxes until that checkbox cluster is migrated.
- `dashboard-controller.ts` no longer writes `dom.dashboardQuery.value`; the controlled Base UI `Input` reads query from `DashboardSearchControlsState`.
- The dashboard card render key now includes active menu, selection, expanded tags, copy feedback, Speed Dial state, and deleting state so React view state is republished when transient card state changes.
- `InlineMenu`, `InlineMenuList`, and `Popover` wrappers now expose the Base UI `nativeButton` path where native `button` render targets are used, removing the Base UI warning during dashboard menu/search-help interactions.

Validation after this slice:

- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100` with no issues.
- `npm run build` passes with the known mixed dynamic/static import warning for `src/popup/search-lookup.ts`.
- `git diff --check` reports only known CRLF conversion warnings.
- A targeted Playwright dashboard smoke with Chrome API and bookmark tree stubs confirmed search query/clear, selection bar select/clear, Base UI card menu open, tag-editor edit/save, no `data-dashboard-action`, no `data-dashboard-toggle-menu`, and no `data-react-island-host`.

#### Options Dashboard Tag Editor Panel Position Slice

Completed after the tag-editor close focus return cleanup:

- `DashboardTagEditorState` now carries `bookmarkId`, `closing`, and `positionRequestId` alongside the existing visible/content/action state.
- `DashboardPanel` owns the `#dashboard-tag-editor` shell ref, visibility class, `aria-hidden`, closing class, and inline `left`/`top`/`right` position style.
- `DashboardResults` now accepts a card element registrar; rendered static and virtual dashboard cards register their article refs so the tag editor can be positioned from React-owned refs.
- The previous controller-owned `positionDashboardTagEditor()` helper was removed. The positioning math now runs in a `useLayoutEffect` from `DashboardPanel`, preserving the previous viewport clamping behavior without controller `getBoundingClientRect()` or inline style writes.
- `dashboard-controller.ts` no longer writes `dom.dashboardTagEditor.classList`, `aria-hidden`, or style. It publishes tag-editor visibility/closing/position requests through `publishDashboardViewState()`.
- Removed stale `dom.dashboardTagEditor`, `dom.dashboardTagEditorTitle`, `dom.dashboardTagEditorMeta`, `dom.dashboardTagEditorField`, `dom.dashboardTagEditorStatus`, and `dom.dashboardTagEditorActions` cache entries from `shared-options/dom.ts`.

#### Options Dashboard Drag Overlay Visual-State Slice

Completed after the tag-editor panel position cleanup:

- `DashboardDragOverlayState` now carries `visible`, `closing`, `moving`, `deleteTargetActive`, and `previewTransform` alongside the existing drag hint, preview, and drop target view models.
- `DashboardPanel` owns `#dashboard-drag-overlay` visibility classes, closing/moving classes, `aria-hidden`, delete-target `active`, panel `is-dashboard-dragging`, and the drag preview inline `transform` through React view state.
- `dashboard-controller.ts` no longer mutates `dom.dashboardDragOverlay.classList`, `dom.dashboardDragOverlay.aria-hidden`, `dom.dashboardDeleteDropTarget.classList`, `dom.dashboardFolderDropGrid` cards, or `dom.dashboardDragPreview.style.transform`.
- The previous overlay close path using `closeWithExitMotion()` was replaced with a tokenized React-state close timer that preserves the 220 ms exit delay and `prefers-reduced-motion` behavior without storing transition tokens on DOM nodes.
- Removed stale `dom.dashboardDragOverlay`, `dom.dashboardDeleteDropTarget`, `dom.dashboardFolderDropGrid`, `dom.dashboardDragPreview`, and `dom.dashboardDragHint` cache entries from `shared-options/dom.ts`.
- At this point in the historical sequence, drag hit detection still used `document.elementFromPoint()` as a deliberate compatibility boundary; the later dashboard drag/drop hit-detection cleanup below moved that boundary into React overlay and folder-card pointer events.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was read before the change; no specific Base UI primitive docs were required because the slice did not alter primitive composition.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- Residual source searches confirm no dashboard drag overlay node refs remain in `shared-options/dom.ts`, and `dashboard-controller.ts` no longer writes overlay class names, `aria-hidden`, delete target active class, drop target active class, or preview transform directly.
- A targeted Playwright dashboard drag smoke with Chrome API and bookmark tree stubs confirmed `#dashboard-drag-overlay` opens under parent `#dashboard`, reports `aria-hidden="false"` while visible, applies a React-owned preview transform, marks a folder drop card `active` through React state, and clears to `hidden` / `aria-hidden="true"` / transform `none` after Escape. The smoke also confirmed no old dashboard marker or island-host nodes are present.

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was read before the change; no specific Base UI primitive docs were required because the slice did not alter primitive composition.

Follow-up cleanup in the same dashboard chrome area:

- Removed the unused selection composite-motion code path after confirming `shouldUseDashboardSelectionCompositeMotion()` always returns `false`.
- Deleted the unused selection motion frame/timer state, deferred virtual resize flag, transition constant, and dead helpers that wrote `is-selection-motion-*` classes or `--dashboard-selection-motion-shift` inline styles.
- Virtual resize now schedules directly instead of checking the removed composite-motion guard.
- `DashboardPanelChromeState` now carries `resultsStableHeight`, `resultsUpdating`, and `resultsVirtualized` for the dashboard result grid.
- `DashboardPanel` now derives `#dashboard-results.is-updating`, `#dashboard-results.is-virtualized`, `--dashboard-results-stable-height`, and the card height/min-width CSS variables from React view state.
- `dashboard-controller.ts` no longer writes or reads `dom.dashboardResults.classList` for `is-virtualized`; controller virtualization logic now reads the same React-published panel chrome state that drives the rendered class.
- `DashboardPanel` now sets the constant dashboard card height/min-width CSS variables on `#dashboard-results` through React inline style, replacing the controller-owned `syncDashboardGridSurface()` DOM style writes.
- `DashboardFaviconShell` in `DashboardView.tsx` now owns favicon image load state locally and dispatches typed `favicon-load` / `favicon-error` React events.
- `dashboard-controller.ts` now tracks failed favicon render keys, removes failed cached remote favicon entries, and schedules a React list rerender so the view model can fall back to the Chrome favicon URL or text fallback.
- Removed the dashboard panel capture-phase favicon `load` / `error` event bindings, the lazy wrapper exports for those handlers, the old `data-dashboard-favicon*` image markers, and the DOM image query/mutation path that rewrote `src`, `data-dashboard-favicon-source`, or `has-favicon`.
- `DashboardPanel` now anchors the Base UI tag editor Popover with the existing `tagEditorRef` instead of `document.getElementById('dashboard-tag-editor')`.
- `DashboardCardContents` now keeps a React ref for the hidden-tag toggle and passes it to `DashboardTagPopoverShell`; the Base UI tag popover Positioner now anchors from that ref instead of `document.getElementById(...)`.
- Closing an open dashboard card menu with Escape now restores focus through the existing React `cardMenuFocusRequest` state rather than querying the rendered card menu trigger.
- Removed the now-unused controller DOM helpers for dashboard card lookup/menu-trigger lookup and the stale tag-popover exit-motion query path.
- `DashboardPanel` has been split into smaller local React components for the toolbar, selection group, results section, tag editor layer, and drag overlay so the results observer migration does not regress React Doctor maintainability.
- Dashboard Escape handling now flows through the React-owned options document `keydown` event store, and dashboard drag start now flows through the same React-owned options document pointer event store as drag move/up/cancel.
- The temporary typed `options:dashboard-panel-event` bridge has been removed, `options-controller.ts` no longer attaches `handleDashboardKeydown` or `handleDashboardPointerDown` directly to `dom.dashboardPanel`, and `shared-options/dom.ts` no longer caches `dom.dashboardPanel`.
- `DashboardResultsSection` now owns the `#dashboard-results` ref, React `onScroll` handler, and `ResizeObserver` lifecycle. It dispatches typed `results-mounted`, `results-scroll`, `results-resize`, and `results-unmounted` dashboard view actions only while the dashboard panel is active.
- `DashboardResultsSection` now includes `clientWidth`, `clientHeight`, `scrollHeight`, and `scrollTop` in mounted/resize/scroll payloads, and `dashboard-controller.ts` stores those values as `virtualState.resultsMetrics`.
- Dashboard virtualization now uses React-published result metrics and scrollTop snapshots for initial virtual-window computation, resize checks, scroll prefetch, and scroll-window rendering.
- Programmatic dashboard result scrolling now flows through `resultsScrollRequest` in `dashboard-view-store.ts`; `DashboardResultsSection` applies the requested scroll position with its own ref/effect and reports a `results-scroll-sync` metrics snapshot back to the controller.
- `renderDashboardListOnly()` now uses the dashboard entry/exit lifecycle flag instead of requiring `dom.dashboardResults`, and stable result-height masking now uses only the React-published `clientHeight` snapshot.
- `dashboard-controller.ts` no longer registers or removes a controller-owned `scroll` listener on `dom.dashboardResults`, and it no longer stores a controller-owned `ResizeObserver` or observed result element.
- `dashboard-controller.ts` no longer imports `shared-options/dom.ts`, no longer reads or writes `dom.dashboardResults`, and the shared options DOM cache no longer stores `dom.dashboardResults`.
- `DashboardDragOverlay` now owns delete-target hover events, `DashboardFolderDropGrid` cards now dispatch typed folder hover events, and `dashboard-controller.ts` no longer calls `document.elementFromPoint()` for dashboard drag/drop hit detection.
- Drag start releases the original card pointer capture once the overlay is active, so the React overlay can receive pointer hover events while the document-level pointermove handler continues to update the preview position.
- The old `src/options/shared-options/dom.ts` cache module has been deleted. `options-controller.ts` no longer calls `cacheDom()`, and residual searches confirm no source imports `shared-options/dom`, `cacheDom`, or `dom.*`.
- Dashboard virtual scroll prefetch now consumes a React-provided scroll metrics snapshot instead of a DOM element reference.
- Dashboard favicon warmup/dirty sync now uses dashboard entry/exit state instead of reading `dom.dashboardPanel.hidden` or requiring `dom.dashboardResults` as a visibility guard.
- Removed the unused `getDashboardRenderedIds()` helper and its stale dashboard-card `querySelectorAll` path.
- `renderDashboardSection()` and `syncDashboardSelectionOnly()` now use the dashboard entry/exit lifecycle flag instead of treating `dom.dashboardResults` existence as an activity guard.
- Remaining dashboard cleanup is now focused on rendered-id reconciliation, deeper virtual-list hook/ref extraction, and first-paint/media bootstrap audits; result metrics, resize/scroll listener ownership, stable height, programmatic scroll positioning, and drag/drop hover hit detection are React-owned.

Validation after this follow-up:

- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- `npm run build` passes with the known mixed dynamic/static import warning for `src/popup/search-lookup.ts`.
- `git diff --check` reports only known CRLF conversion warnings.
- A targeted Playwright dashboard smoke confirmed dashboard ready state, layout motion mode, selection toggling, and no old dashboard marker or island-host nodes.
- A targeted Playwright dashboard CSS-variable smoke confirmed `#dashboard-results` receives React-owned inline `--dashboard-card-height: 150px` and `--dashboard-card-min-width: 260px`, overriding the remaining legacy CSS defaults without controller style mutation.
- Targeted Playwright dashboard static-list and virtual-list smokes with Chrome API and bookmark tree stubs confirmed the static list renders without `is-virtualized`, the large list renders `#dashboard-results.is-virtualized` and a virtual window, scroll state remains functional, old `data-dashboard-favicon` image markers are absent, there are no JS errors, and no `data-react-island-host` nodes remain.
- A targeted Playwright dashboard tag-popover/menu smoke with Chrome API, bookmark tree, and tag-index stubs confirmed the hidden-tag Popover opens and renders all tags, the ref-anchored positioner is present, Escape closes an open card menu and returns focus to the matching menu trigger through React state, there are no JS errors, and no `data-react-island-host` nodes remain.
- A targeted Playwright dashboard results observer smoke with Chrome API and bookmark tree stubs confirmed a 24-item static list renders all cards without virtualization, a 520-item large list renders 51 initial virtual cards, scrolling updates the virtual window from bookmark `1000` to `1009`, old favicon markers are absent, no island hosts remain, and no runtime page errors occur.
- Follow-up validation after removing the stale rendered-id query and DOM visibility guards: `npm run typecheck`, `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff`, `npm run build`, and the same targeted dashboard static/virtual Playwright smoke all pass. The build still reports only the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- Follow-up validation after replacing result-DOM activity guards in render/selection paths: `npm run typecheck`, `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff`, `npm run build`, and the targeted dashboard static/virtual Playwright smoke all pass. The build warning remains the same known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- Follow-up validation after moving dashboard root keydown/pointerdown ownership into React: `npm run typecheck`, `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff`, and `npm run build` all pass. The build warning remains the same known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning. A targeted Playwright dashboard event smoke with Chrome API, bookmark tree, storage, permissions, runtime, tabs, commands, and notification stubs confirmed the Base UI card menu opens and closes on Escape, dragging a card starts the React-owned drag overlay, Escape hides the overlay, three cards render, no old dashboard action/favicon markers remain, and no `data-react-island-host` nodes remain.
- Follow-up validation after moving dashboard virtual metrics into React event payloads: `npm run typecheck`, `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff`, and `npm run build` all pass. The build warning remains the same known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning. A targeted Playwright dashboard virtual-list smoke with 520 bookmark stubs confirmed `#dashboard-results.is-virtualized`, 68 initial rendered cards, scroll metrics captured from React payloads, scrolling to `scrollTop=8000` updates the first rendered bookmark from `1000` to `1152` with 116 rendered cards, no old dashboard markers remain, and no `data-react-island-host` nodes remain.
- Follow-up validation after preferring React metrics for stable-height masking and list-only activity checks: `npm run typecheck`, `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff`, `npm run build`, and the targeted 520-bookmark dashboard virtual-list smoke all pass. The build warning remains the same known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- Follow-up validation after moving dashboard programmatic result scrolling into React scroll requests: `npm run typecheck`, `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff`, and `npm run build` all pass. The build warning remains the same known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning. A targeted 520-bookmark Playwright dashboard virtual-list smoke confirmed `#dashboard-results.is-virtualized`, 72 initial rendered cards, scrolling to `scrollTop=8000` updates the first rendered bookmark from `1000` to `1152` with 120 rendered cards, no old dashboard markers remain, no `data-react-island-host` nodes remain, the Base UI card menu opens and closes on Escape, and no page errors occur.
- Follow-up validation after moving dashboard drag/drop hit detection into React overlay events: `npm run typecheck`, `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff`, and `npm run build` all pass. The build warning remains the same known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning. Targeted Playwright dashboard drag smokes confirmed dragging bookmark `1000` to folder `11` activates the folder target and calls `chrome.bookmarks.move`, dragging over the delete target activates the delete drop affordance, Escape cancels and hides the overlay, no old dashboard markers remain, and no `data-react-island-host` nodes remain.
- Follow-up validation after deleting the old options DOM cache module: residual searches confirm no `shared-options/dom`, `cacheDom`, or `dom.*` references remain in `src/options`; `npm run typecheck`, `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff`, and `npm run build` all pass. The build warning remains the same known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.

#### Options Route/Nav Ownership Slice

Completed after the newtab background media layer slice:

- Added `src/options/options-section-store.ts` so React and the controller share section parsing, embed detection, and a typed `curator:options-section-change` notification.
- `OptionsApp` now owns section link navigation clicks, active nav classes, `aria-current`, panel `hidden` state, and dashboard shell/embed classes from React state.
- Options panels now receive explicit `hidden` props instead of relying on `options-controller.ts` to scan `[data-section-panel]`.
- The old controller-owned navigation DOM pass was removed: no more `document.querySelectorAll('[data-section-link]')`, `document.querySelectorAll('[data-section-panel]')`, active-link `classList` toggling, or panel `hidden` writes in `syncPageSection`.
- The availability nav group remains a Base UI controlled `Collapsible` owned by React; the old controller collapsible helper with `querySelector`, `classList`, inline max-height/opacity writes, and click delegation was removed.
- The dashboard embed root visual state now keys off `.options-shell.options-dashboard-embed` with `:has(...)` CSS instead of controller-written `html.options-dashboard-embed-root`.
- `options-controller.ts` still owns section-change business effects such as dashboard entry/exit, large repository hydration, tag section render/unmount, AI section render, timers, title updates, and anchor scrolling.

Validation after this slice:

- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warnings about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import and large chunks.
- `git diff --check` reports only CRLF working copy warnings.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- A targeted Playwright options route/nav smoke with Chrome API and bookmark tree stubs confirmed overview and availability panel visibility is React-owned, availability nav active state and collapsible visibility update correctly, dashboard fullscreen classes live on `.options-shell`, `body.dashboard-fullscreen-active` and `html.options-dashboard-embed-root` remain absent, section-store navigation back to General clears the dashboard shell class, embed mode shows the dashboard through `.options-shell.options-dashboard-embed.dashboard-fullscreen-active`, and filtered console warnings/errors remain 0.

#### Options Empty-State CTA Slice

Completed after the options route/nav ownership slice:

- Empty-state CTA navigation moved into React-owned button handlers through `navigateToOptionsEmptyStateAction`.
- `options-controller.ts` no longer registers a document-level click delegate for `[data-empty-cta]`.
- `options-section-store.ts` now centralizes route hash navigation for both ordinary section links and empty-state actions.
- The bookmark add history UI was thinned to a renderer only; the header, summary, list, card, and state types moved into normal component/type modules. This was superseded by the later direct-render cleanup.
- The bookmark add history island no longer uses `flushSync`, and its previous barrel imports were replaced with direct primitive/theme imports.

Validation after this slice:

- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- A targeted Playwright options empty-state CTA smoke with Chrome API and bookmark tree stubs confirmed the bookmark-history empty-state `配置 AI 渠道` button navigates to `#ai`, the AI panel becomes visible, and filtered console warnings/errors remain 0.

Completed after the options empty-state CTA slice:

- `options-controller.ts` now dispatches typed AI config link state instead of writing `textContent`, toggling `classList`, or setting attributes on `#ai-config-link`.
- `shared-options/dom.ts` no longer caches `dom.aiConfigLink`.
- The link uses the shared options section navigation helper to preserve `#general:ai-provider` routing and the existing AI provider attention highlight.

#### Options AI Analysis Status Card Slice

Completed after the options AI config link slice:

- Added `AiAnalysisStatusCard` and `ai-analysis-status-store.ts` so the AI analysis execution status card owns `#ai-run-badge` text/tone and `#ai-status-copy` in React.
- `options-controller.ts` now publishes computed AI analysis status through a small external store instead of writing `className` and `textContent` to the badge and copy nodes.
- `shared-options/dom.ts` no longer caches `dom.aiRunBadge` or `dom.aiStatusCopy`.
- The status store keeps the current snapshot so controller updates are not lost if they occur before the component effect phase.
