#### Newtab Drag Shell Slice

Completed after the newtab app chrome class slice:

- Drag UI classes now derive from `useNewtabDragUiView` inside `useNewtabAppChromeAttributes` and apply to the React-owned `.newtab-app` wrapper.
- `NewtabBodyClassesHost` was removed; newtab no longer mutates `document.body.classList` for bookmark, speed-dial, folder-order, or preview-initializing drag state.
- `NewtabShell` now renders `#newtab-drag-layer`, and `mountNewTabDragGhostBridge` appends drag ghosts only to that React-owned layer instead of falling back to `document.body`.
- Drag CSS selectors now target `.newtab-app.*` instead of `body.*`, while drag ghost styling still applies to the cloned ghost elements.
- A follow-up cleanup removed the last `document.body` fallback from `mountNewTabDragGhostBridge`; if the React drag layer is unavailable, ghost creation now aborts instead of escaping React ownership.

Validation after this slice:

- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warnings about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import and large chunks.
- `git diff --check` reports only CRLF working copy warnings.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- A targeted Playwright newtab drag/class smoke confirmed drag classes apply to `.newtab-app`, `body` remains free of drag classes, `#newtab-drag-layer` is present and fixed, a ghost mounted there retains fixed positioning and `pointer-events: none`, and filtered console warnings remain 0.
- Follow-up verification after removing the fallback: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, `git diff --check`, and `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` still pass; searches confirm no `document.body` ghost fallback remains.

#### Newtab Background Media Layer Slice

Completed after the newtab drag shell slice:

- `NewtabShell` now renders `NewtabBackgroundLayer` inside the React-owned `.newtab-app` wrapper.
- `src/newtab/newtab-background-media-store.ts` exposes the current background media as React-consumed state.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer applies runtime background image, size, or position through `document.body.style`.
- Runtime background videos are no longer created, prepended to `document.body`, or removed through DOM selectors; React renders the live `<video>` element and the controller only probes metadata before publishing the video source.
- Background mask style and blur now come from React-owned `.newtab-app` attributes/styles instead of controller-written DOM attributes.
- `.newtab-background-image` and `.newtab-background-video` now share the full-screen fixed background layer rules in `newtab.css`.
- The instant-wallpaper bootstrap CSS and `html` readiness classes remain in place for first-paint caching and loading transitions.

Validation after this slice:

- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warnings about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import and large chunks.
- `git diff --check` reports only CRLF working copy warnings.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- A targeted Playwright newtab background media smoke with Chrome API, bookmark tree, storage, permission, and tab stubs confirmed `.newtab-app > .newtab-background-image` receives the configured URL, background mask state/style/blur live on `.newtab-app`, `body` has no runtime inline background image/size/position, no background video is mounted directly under `body`, and filtered console warnings/errors remain 0.

#### Newtab Icon Layout Store Slice

Completed after the newtab background media layer slice:

- `newtab-content-store.ts` now exposes `patchNewtabContentView` so page-level React state can be patched without re-rendering through controller DOM writes.
- `newtab-controller.ts` now builds bookmark icon layout view state through `createBookmarkContentStyleState`.
- `createBookmarkSections` and `applyIconSettingsLive` share the same icon layout view-model calculation, so initial render and live settings updates publish identical React state.
- `applyIconSettingsLive` no longer queries `.newtab-content` or `.newtab-page` to write `--icon-*` CSS variables, `data-icon-layout-mode`, `data-icon-show-titles`, or `data-icon-vertical-center`.
- Page-level vertical-center state now updates through `patchNewtabContentView`, and content-level icon CSS variables update through `patchNewtabBookmarkContentView`.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer caches `#newtab-root`, `#newtab-dashboard-trigger`, `#newtab-settings-trigger`, or `#newtab-settings-backdrop` at module load. These React-rendered shell elements are refreshed when the controller starts, and the drawer/dashboard initializers re-read the relevant trigger/backdrop nodes after React mount.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before implementation and verification. The slice kept the existing Base UI settings controls and did not touch a specific primitive API.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- Residual searches confirm the old icon-layout controller writes to `.newtab-content` and `.newtab-page` are gone.
- A targeted production-build Playwright newtab smoke with Chrome API, bookmark tree, storage, permission, and tab stubs confirmed 4 bookmark tiles render, compact preset changes React-owned `--icon-page-width` from `1498px` to `1382px` and `--icon-tile-width` from `184px` to `148px`, and toggling vertical center changes both `.newtab-content` and `.newtab-page` `data-icon-vertical-center` to `true` without page errors.

#### Newtab Search Layout Store Slice

Completed after the newtab icon layout store slice:

- `content-state.ts` now carries `primaryCollisionOffsetY` on `NewTabPageView`, initialized by `createNewTabPage`.
- `NewtabContentHost.tsx` applies `--primary-collision-offset-y` through the React-owned `.newtab-page` style instead of controller-owned `page.style.setProperty` / `removeProperty`.
- `applySearchSettingsLive` now computes normalized search settings and patches `NewtabSearchWidget` shell state instead of writing `--search-width`, `--search-height`, `--search-offset-y`, `--search-bg-alpha`, or `data-search-auto-vertical-center` on slot/form DOM nodes.
- `runBatchedAdaptiveLayoutUpdate` still measures the live layout, but now publishes the resulting search width/offset and collision offset through `patchSearchWidgetShellState` and `patchNewtabPageCollisionOffset`.
- `getCurrentSearchOffsetY` reads the registered React search widget shell state before falling back to stored settings, so adaptive layout no longer depends on an inline DOM style read.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before implementation and verification. The slice kept the existing Base UI settings controls and did not touch a specific primitive API.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- Residual searches confirm no `slot.style.setProperty('--search-*')`, `form?.style.setProperty('--search-*')`, `dataset.searchAutoVerticalCenter`, or `.newtab-page` `--primary-collision-offset-y` style writes remain in `src/newtab/newtab-controller.ts`.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- A targeted production-build Playwright newtab smoke with Chrome API, bookmark tree, storage, permission, and tab stubs confirmed 4 bookmark tiles render, initial search shell styles are `44vw` / `40px` / `0px` / `0.3`, live settings updates change React-owned search styles to `60vw` / `48px` / `40px` / `0.7`, auto vertical-center switches `data-search-auto-vertical-center` to `true`, and `.newtab-page` owns `--primary-collision-offset-y` via React style without page errors.

#### Newtab Drag Active-Class Store Slice

Completed after the newtab search layout store slice:

- `newtab-speed-dial-store.ts` now exposes `patchNewtabSpeedDialView` so drag-only state can update the existing Speed Dial panel without a full controller render.
- `beginSpeedDialDrag`, `beginBookmarkDrag`, and `beginFolderDrag` now patch React view state for the active card/tile/folder `dragging` marker after ghost cloning and layout capture.
- `clearSpeedDialDragState`, `clearBookmarkDragState`, and `clearFolderDragState` clear those markers through the same React stores instead of querying active DOM nodes and calling `classList.remove('dragging')`.
- The drag ghost compatibility layer remains in place for this slice; it still clones the source node, clears `dragging` from the clone, and mounts the preview under the React-rendered `#newtab-drag-layer`.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before implementation and verification. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- Residual searches confirm the controller no longer calls `classList.add('dragging')` or `classList.remove('dragging')` on source speed-dial cards, bookmark tiles, or folder headers. Remaining `ghost.classList.remove('dragging')` calls are limited to clone cleanup in the temporary ghost bridge.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- A targeted production-build Playwright newtab drag smoke with Chrome API, bookmark tree, storage, permission, tab, Speed Dial, and workspace stubs long-pressed a Speed Dial card, a bookmark tile, and a folder header. In all three cases React rendered the `dragging` class during drag, the ghost mounted under `#newtab-drag-layer`, releasing removed the class, and the ghost was removed without page errors.

#### Newtab Drag Ghost React Layer Slice

Completed after the newtab drag active-class store slice:

- `newtab-drag-layer-store.ts` now carries Speed Dial, bookmark tile, and folder drag ghost view state.
- `NewtabDragLayerHost.tsx` renders `.speed-dial-drag-ghost`, `.bookmark-drag-ghost`, and `.folder-drag-ghost` directly from React state inside `#newtab-drag-layer`.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer creates drag ghost DOM clones, clears clone classes, appends ghost nodes, mutates ghost visibility, or removes ghost elements. It now publishes ghost view models and patches transforms through the drag layer store.
- Bookmark tile ghosts reuse React bookmark tile view-model data and copy only the computed CSS variables needed to preserve icon/card sizing and coloring while the ghost is portaled outside the content tree.
- Folder ghosts reuse the active folder section title/count and preserve the existing center-pointer drag offset.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before implementation and verification. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- Residual searches confirm `bookmarkDragGhost`, `folderDragGhost`, `mountNewTabDragGhostBridge`, and `cloneNode(true)` are gone from newtab controller code; remaining drag ghost class names are React-rendered components and CSS selectors.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- A targeted production-build Playwright newtab drag smoke with Chrome API, bookmark tree, storage, permission, tab, Speed Dial, and workspace stubs long-pressed a Speed Dial card, a bookmark tile, and a folder header. In all three cases React rendered the active source `dragging` class, the matching ghost rendered under `#newtab-drag-layer` with a matrix transform while dragging, release removed the class and ghost, and page/runtime console errors were 0.

#### Newtab Drag Preview Transform Store Slice

Completed after the newtab drag ghost React layer slice:

- Speed Dial reorder preview transforms and z-index markers are now merged into React-owned `SpeedDialCardViewModel.style` via `patchNewtabSpeedDialView`.
- Bookmark reorder preview transforms and z-index markers are now merged into React-owned `BookmarkTileViewModel.style` via `patchNewtabBookmarkContentView`.
- `clearSpeedDialDragVisualPreview` and `clearBookmarkDragVisualPreview` now clear only React view-model preview style keys, preserving existing favicon accent CSS variables.
- The controller still performs layout measurement through DOM rect snapshots for drag hit testing and FLIP-style reorder projection, but it no longer writes preview `transform` or `zIndex` directly to card/tile DOM elements.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before implementation and verification. The slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- Residual searches confirm there are no `style.transform`, `style.zIndex`, `card.style`, `tile.style`, `ghost.style`, `cloneNode(true)`, or `mountNewTabDragGhostBridge` references in `src/newtab/newtab-controller.ts`.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- A targeted production-build Playwright newtab drag smoke confirmed Speed Dial and bookmark source elements receive React-rendered inline preview `transform`/`z-index` during drag, those preview keys are removed after release while accent CSS variables remain, all three drag ghosts still render under `#newtab-drag-layer`, and page/runtime console errors were 0.

#### Newtab Settings Drawer And Featured Picker Store Slice

Completed after the newtab drag preview transform store slice:

- `SettingsDrawer` now passes `modal={false}` to the Base UI Drawer root while keeping `disablePointerDismissal`, matching the documented non-modal Drawer pattern and preventing the hidden Base UI inert presentation layer from intercepting settings tab clicks inside the open drawer.
- The drawer still uses the existing React-owned custom backdrop, explicit close action, and controller focus trap behavior, so the user-facing settings workflow remains modal-like without relying on Base UI page inerting.
- `newtab-featured-background-picker-store.ts` now exposes a read-only picker view snapshot.
- `openFeaturedBackgroundPickerContent` and the featured picker render-signature fast path now check whether React picker sections contain cards instead of querying `.featured-wallpaper-card` in the live DOM.
- `BackgroundSettingsSection` moved its file-select dispatcher helper to module scope to keep React Doctor diff diagnostics clean.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/drawer`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/tabs`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog` were read before implementation and verification.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- Residual searches confirm the featured picker rendered-content check no longer queries `.featured-wallpaper-card`; remaining featured card DOM queries are focused on initial focus and preview image observation/hydration.
- A targeted production-build Playwright newtab smoke opened the settings drawer, clicked the Base UI Tabs appearance tab without inert interception, opened the controlled Base UI featured gallery dialog, rendered 12 `.featured-wallpaper-card` nodes from the React picker store, confirmed `aria-hidden="false"`, confirmed no `data-react-island-host` nodes, and reported 0 page/runtime console errors.

#### Newtab Featured Picker Initial Focus Slice

Completed after the newtab settings drawer and featured picker store slice:

- `newtab-featured-background-picker-store.ts` now has a monotonically increasing initial-focus request store.
- `focusFeaturedBackgroundPickerInitialTarget()` no longer queries `.featured-wallpaper-card.is-selected` or the first `.featured-wallpaper-card`; it only publishes the React focus request when the controlled featured picker dialog is open.
- `FeaturedBackgroundPicker` resolves the selected-card-or-first-card focus target from the picker view model and each card focuses itself through its own ref/effect.
- `FeaturedBackgroundModal` owns the no-card fallback by focusing the close button through a React ref when a focus request arrives while the picker has no focusable cards.
- Featured wallpaper cards now use the Base UI Button composition with `nativeButton={false}` and `unstyled`, preserving the existing card DOM shape for nested favorite buttons without leaking default app button styling.
- `CloseButton` accepts React 19 ref-as-prop so the modal fallback focus path remains React-owned without reintroducing `forwardRef` diagnostics.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md` were read before implementation and verification.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- Residual searches confirm the featured picker initial-focus path no longer queries `.featured-wallpaper-card`; remaining featured picker DOM queries are now preview image observation/hydration and hover-preview card dataset reads.
- A targeted production-build Playwright newtab smoke with Chrome API, bookmark tree, storage, permission, and remote image stubs opened the settings drawer, switched to the Base UI appearance tab, opened the controlled Base UI featured gallery dialog, rendered 12 `.featured-wallpaper-card` nodes, focused the selected React card `nasa-apollo-earthrise`, confirmed `aria-hidden="false"`, confirmed no `data-react-island-host` nodes, confirmed no default Button styling leaked onto wallpaper cards, and reported no console/request errors.

#### Newtab Featured Picker Preview Hydration Slice

Completed after the newtab featured picker initial-focus slice:

- `FeaturedBackgroundPickerCard` now owns preview thumbnail hydration with React refs/effects instead of controller-scanned `.featured-wallpaper-card` nodes.
- Initial preview hydration is driven by the card view model `renderIndex`; below-the-fold cards use a React-owned `IntersectionObserver` rooted to the modal grid ref.
- Preview object URL resolution is passed as a typed view-model callback, so card images update through React reducer state instead of controller-written `img.src`.
- Image fallback and resolved preview URL tracking now live in card-local refs/reducer state rather than `data-featured-background-*` or image `data-*` attributes.
- Hover preview scheduling now receives a typed React payload with `element`, `imageUrl`, `title`, and `resolvedPreviewUrl`; the controller only keeps the existing delay, object-URL upgrade, and element measurement needed for preview placement.
- Removed the now-unused `src/newtab/featured-gallery-preview-registry.ts` compatibility registry.

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before implementation and verification. The existing Button/Dialog composition was preserved.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- Residual searches confirm no featured preview registry, preview observer functions, `data-featured-background-preview-*`, `data-remote-preview-url`, or `data-preview-fallback-urls` paths remain.
- A targeted production-build Playwright newtab smoke with Chrome API, bookmark tree, storage, permission, and image stubs opened the settings drawer, switched to the Base UI appearance tab, opened the controlled Base UI featured gallery dialog, rendered 12 `.featured-wallpaper-card` nodes, focused the selected React card `nasa-apollo-earthrise`, showed the hover preview, confirmed `aria-hidden="false"`, confirmed no `data-react-island-host` nodes, confirmed no old preview dataset nodes, confirmed the selected card remains a Base UI-composed `DIV`, and reported no actionable console/request errors.

#### Newtab Source Folder Candidate Focus Slice

Completed after the newtab featured picker preview hydration slice:

- `newtab-folder-source-store.ts` now exposes a typed folder-candidate focus request store consumed through `useNewtabFolderCandidateFocusRequest`.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer queries `[data-folder-candidate-id]`, mutates candidate `tabIndex`, reads `dataset.folderCandidateId`, or writes candidate-list `scrollTop` while handling source-folder candidate keyboard movement.
- The controller computes the target candidate from React-published filtered candidate data and dispatches a typed focus request.
- `SettingsDrawer` owns the source-folder search input ref and candidate button refs, then performs focus and scroll alignment from React effects.
- Candidate rows no longer render `data-folder-candidate-id`, removing the old DOM selector contract.
- Candidate/search keydown handlers stop propagation after controller handling, preserving Escape behavior without conflicting with the drawer-level keyboard handling.

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/input` were re-read before implementation and verification. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Button/Input wrappers.

- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- A targeted production-build Playwright newtab smoke confirmed expanding source-folder candidates focuses `#folder-candidate-search`, `ArrowDown` focuses the first candidate, `End` moves to the last candidate, `Escape` returns focus to the search input, `data-folder-candidate-id` nodes are gone, and no `data-react-island-host` nodes render.

#### Newtab Settings Drawer Focus And Scroll Request Slice

Completed after the newtab source folder candidate focus slice:

- `newtab-settings-drawer-store.ts` now exposes a typed layout request store for settings drawer `focus-first-control`, `focus-section`, and `scroll-top` actions.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer queries settings drawer sections to focus the first control, no longer queries `#folder-candidate-search` for source-section focus, and no longer queries `.settings-drawer-scroll` to reset tab scroll position.
- `SettingsDrawer` owns the scroll container ref, section refs, and source-folder search input ref, then executes layout requests through React effects.
- The existing Base UI Drawer/Tabs composition remains unchanged; the controller only publishes intent after opening the drawer or changing the active settings group.
- The remaining settings drawer DOM focus trap is still a controller-owned compatibility path and should be audited separately.

#### Newtab Settings Drawer Tab Focus Trap Slice

Completed after the newtab settings drawer focus/scroll request slice:

- The settings drawer `Tab` wrap behavior moved from the controller global keydown path into `SettingsDrawer`.
- `SettingsDrawer` now registers a document-level `keydown` listener only while the drawer is open, the panel ref exists, and the featured background modal is not open.
- The React component reuses its own panel ref and local focusable-element helper to wrap `Shift+Tab` from the first control to the last control, wrap `Tab` from the last control back to the close button, and pull focus back inside the drawer if focus leaves while tabbing.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer calls `trapSettingsDrawerFocus`; the remaining controller `getFocusableElements` helper now serves only the featured background modal and dashboard overlay compatibility traps.
- The existing controlled non-modal Base UI Drawer and controlled Tabs composition remains unchanged.

#### Newtab Featured Background Modal Tab Focus Trap Slice

Completed after the newtab settings drawer Tab focus trap slice:

- `DialogPanel` now accepts the normal React `ref` prop and forwards it to the Base UI `Dialog.Popup`, preserving the existing app wrapper while letting callers keep panel refs without DOM queries.
- `FeaturedBackgroundModal` owns the featured gallery dialog panel ref and `Tab` wrap listener in React.
- The modal registers its keydown listener only while the controlled featured gallery dialog is open and the panel ref exists.

#### Newtab Dashboard Overlay Tab Focus Trap Slice

Completed after the newtab featured background modal Tab focus trap slice:

- `DialogOverlay` now accepts the normal React `ref` prop and forwards it to the Base UI Dialog portal wrapper `div`, mirroring the existing `DialogPanel` ref support.
- `DashboardOverlay` owns the dashboard overlay root ref and `Tab` wrap listener in React.
- The overlay registers its keydown listener only while the controlled dashboard dialog is open and the overlay ref exists.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer branches on `event.key === 'Tab'` in the global document keydown handler.
- `trapDashboardOverlayFocus`, the controller-local `getFocusableElements` helper, and the controller-local `FOCUSABLE_SELECTOR` were removed.
- The controller still owns dashboard hash routing, iframe load/error/ready coordination, message passing, and return-focus bookkeeping while React owns the overlay focus boundary.

#### Newtab Search Layout Measurement Refs Slice

Completed after the newtab dashboard overlay Tab focus trap slice:

- `newtab-content-store.ts` now exposes React-owned content layout nodes: shell, page, utility stack, primary slot, and primary content.
- `NewtabShell` passes a React ref for `#newtab-root` into `NewtabContentHost`.
- `NewtabContentHost` owns refs for the newtab page, utility stack, and primary slot, publishes layout nodes from a layout effect, and emits a typed layout-node change event when those refs mount, change, or unmount.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer queries `.newtab-page`, `.newtab-search-slot`, `.newtab-utility-stack`, `.newtab-primary-slot`, or `.newtab-content` while running the adaptive search layout update.
- The controller still performs necessary `getBoundingClientRect()` measurement from React-published refs, then patches the React-owned search shell and page collision-offset state.
- `getSearchAutoVerticalCenterNextModule` now uses the search slot sibling or the React-owned primary slot first child instead of querying page structure.

#### Newtab Featured Modal Node Refs Slice

Completed after the newtab search layout measurement refs slice:

- `newtab-featured-background-modal-store.ts` now exposes React-owned featured modal nodes for the modal overlay and grid.
- `FeaturedBackgroundModal` publishes its overlay and grid refs through a layout effect and clears them on unmount.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer caches `#background-featured-modal`, `#background-featured-modal-grid`, or `#background-featured-modal-close`.
- Featured modal readiness no longer waits on DOM lookups, avoiding a closed-modal wait loop; picker rendering retries on the next animation frame until the React-owned grid ref is available.
- Featured hover-preview positioning now reads the modal bounds from the React-owned modal ref.
- The remaining `#background-featured-picker` cached fallback is the settings trigger return-focus path and should be audited with the settings trigger ref cleanup.
- The `renderedBookmarkIndex` path was reviewed during this slice and is view-model ordering data for progressive bookmark rendering, not a remaining DOM rendered-id reconciliation contract.

#### Newtab Settings Trigger And Featured Picker Return-Focus Slice

Completed after the newtab featured modal node refs slice:

- `newtab-settings-drawer-store.ts` now exposes React-owned settings drawer nodes for the settings trigger.
- `NewtabShell` publishes the `#newtab-settings-trigger` button through a React ref instead of letting the controller cache it.
- `newtab-featured-background-picker-store.ts` now exposes React-owned featured picker nodes for the picker trigger.
- `BackgroundSettingsSection` publishes the `#background-featured-picker` button through a React ref.
- `SettingsDrawer` passes a function-valued `finalFocus` to the Base UI Drawer popup so drawer close returns focus through the React-published settings trigger.
- `FeaturedBackgroundModal` passes a function-valued `finalFocus` to the Base UI Dialog popup so featured gallery close returns focus through the React-published picker trigger.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer keeps controller-owned settings drawer or featured gallery return-focus element caches, and it no longer focuses those trigger nodes manually after close.

Validation after this slice:

#### Newtab Dashboard Overlay Node Refs Slice

Completed after the newtab settings trigger and featured picker return-focus slice:

- `newtab-dashboard-overlay-store.ts` now exposes React-owned dashboard overlay nodes for the trigger, overlay wrapper, and iframe.
- `NewtabShell` publishes the `#newtab-dashboard-trigger` button through a React ref.
- `DashboardOverlay` publishes the `#newtab-dashboard-overlay` wrapper and `#newtab-dashboard-frame` iframe through callback refs.
- The dashboard overlay node store emits a microtask-delayed node-change event so the controller can continue dashboard loading when Base UI mounts the keep-mounted dialog DOM after an open transition.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer caches `#newtab-dashboard-trigger`, `#newtab-dashboard-overlay`, or `#newtab-dashboard-frame`; dashboard route, focus, iframe `src`, and postMessage coordination now consume React-published refs.

#### Newtab Settings Drawer And Backdrop Node Refs Slice

Completed after the newtab dashboard overlay node refs slice:

- `newtab-settings-drawer-store.ts` now exposes React-owned settings drawer nodes for the trigger, backdrop, and outer drawer wrapper, and emits a microtask-delayed node-change event when refs change.
- `NewtabShell` publishes the `#newtab-settings-backdrop` button through a React ref alongside the settings trigger.
- `SettingsDrawer` publishes the outer `#newtab-settings-drawer` wrapper through `DrawerOverlay`'s `overlayRef`; the inner drawer panel remains a separate React ref for focus trapping and portal placement.
- `DrawerOverlay` now accepts `overlayRef` while preserving the documented Base UI `Drawer.Root > Portal > Viewport > Popup` composition.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer caches `#newtab-settings-backdrop` or `#newtab-settings-drawer`; drawer readiness and the opening transition layout flush consume React-published refs.

#### Newtab Shell Node Event Binding Slice

Completed after the newtab settings drawer/backdrop node refs slice:

- `newtab-content-store.ts` now exposes `setNewtabContentShellNode` so `NewtabShell` can publish the `#newtab-root` shell through a React ref before page content nodes are available.
- `NewtabContentHost` preserves the current React-published shell node when page content mounts or unmounts, while still clearing page, primary slot, primary content, and utility stack refs.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer caches `#newtab-root` and no longer keeps a controller-owned `root` global.
- Shell-level click, pointerdown, and contextmenu delegation now binds through `getNewtabContentLayoutNodes().shell`, with explicit unbind/rebind when the React-published shell node changes.
- The render path now dispatches React content state directly instead of returning early when a controller-cached root node is missing.

- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.

#### Newtab Source Navigation Refs Slice

Completed after the newtab shell node event binding slice:

- `SourceNavigationState` no longer carries a controller callback for source-section focus.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer resolves source navigation clicks with `cachedEl(anchorId)`, and the controller-owned `cachedEl`/`elementByIdCache` helper has been removed.
- `NewtabBookmarkContent` now owns folder section refs and handles source navigation scroll/focus locally from React.
- Source navigation links now render as button-backed controls because they run an in-page focus action rather than browser navigation.
- `BookmarkFolderGrid` was cleaned up while touching this file: the incremental rendering state no longer syncs props through effects, expansion scheduling uses refs/rAF, direct `Button`/`Icon` imports replaced the barrel import, and the loading placeholder now uses native `output`.

#### Newtab Speed Dial Node Refs Slice

Completed after the newtab source navigation refs slice:

- `newtab-speed-dial-store.ts` now exposes React-published Speed Dial nodes for the grid and card elements.
- `NewtabSpeedDialPanel` publishes the grid and each card through React refs, including cleanup on unmount or id changes.
- `newtab-controller.ts` now reads active Speed Dial drag cards, the Speed Dial grid, and Speed Dial layout slots through `getNewtabSpeedDialNodes()` instead of controller-owned global selectors.
- Bookmark-change refresh detection now uses the React Speed Dial view-model item ids instead of querying rendered Speed Dial anchors.
- `SpeedDialItemsGrid` was split so hooks stay outside conditional branches, and the loading state now uses native `output`.

#### Newtab Bookmark And Folder Drag Node Refs Slice

Completed after the newtab Speed Dial node refs slice:

- `BookmarkIconShell` now accepts a React 19 `ref` prop and resets missing-favicon fallback state during render when `favicon.src` changes, avoiding prop-sync effects.
- `newtab-bookmark-content-store.ts` now exposes React-published nodes for bookmark grids, bookmark tiles, bookmark tile icons, folder sections, and folder headers.
- `NewtabBookmarkContent` publishes grid/tile/icon/folder refs from React components and cleans them up on unmount or id changes.
- `newtab-speed-dial-store.ts` and `NewtabSpeedDialPanel` now also publish Speed Dial icon refs so drag offset measurement no longer queries child icon nodes.
- `newtab-controller.ts` now uses React-published refs for active bookmark drag tiles, bookmark drag slot measurement, active bookmark grids, folder drag headers, folder section snapshots, and folder FLIP animation targets.
- The controller no longer uses global selectors for `.bookmark-tile`, `.bookmark-grid`, `.folder-section-header`, or `.bookmark-folder-section` drag measurement/animation paths.

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button` were read before implementation and verification. - `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100` after converting `BookmarkIconShell` away from `forwardRef` and removing its prop-sync effect warning.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.

#### Newtab React-Owned Drag Start Slice

Completed after the bookmark/folder drag node refs slice:

- `SpeedDialCardViewModel`, `BookmarkTileViewModel`, and `BookmarkFolderSectionViewModel` now carry typed `onDragPointerDown` callbacks.
- `NewtabSpeedDialPanel`, `BookmarkTile`, and `FolderSectionHeader` now start drag from their own React `onPointerDown` handlers instead of relying on shell-level delegated `pointerdown`.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer binds Speed Dial, bookmark tile, or folder header drag-start listeners to the React shell.
- Drag start now receives explicit bookmark/folder ids and `currentTarget` elements from React, so the controller no longer discovers drag targets through `event.target.closest('[data-*]')` or reads drag-start ids from `dataset`.
- Global `pointermove`/`pointerup` remain as pointer-orchestration compatibility behavior while the rest of the drag flow is audited.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button` were read before implementation and verification. The Button docs confirm Base UI Button keeps button semantics, `type="submit"` must be explicit for submit behavior, and links should be styled directly as `<a>` rather than rendered through Button. This slice preserves anchors for Speed Dial/bookmark links and keeps folder headers as `type="button"`.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- Residual searches confirm the old `handleSpeedDialPointerDown`, `handleBookmarkPointerDown`, `handleFolderPointerDown`, and shell `pointerdown` drag-start bindings are gone. Remaining `closest('[data-*]')` paths are click suppression and context-menu compatibility, not drag-start ownership.

#### Newtab React-Owned Bookmark Context Menu Entry Slice

- `SpeedDialCardViewModel`, `BookmarkTileViewModel`, and Quick Access item view models now carry typed React `onContextMenu` callbacks.

- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer discovers bookmark menu targets through `target.closest('[data-bookmark-id]')` on the shell context-menu listener.
- Bookmark context-menu entry now receives explicit bookmark ids from React view models while preserving the existing edit menu host and action behavior.

#### Newtab React-Owned Featured Modal Backdrop Slice

Completed after the search context-menu boundary slice:

- `FeaturedBackgroundModal` now owns backdrop pointer handling with a React `onPointerDownCapture` check against `event.currentTarget`, then requests close through the controlled Dialog `onOpenChange(false, event)`.
- The close button path remains a Base UI `Dialog.Close`, so close button, Escape, and backdrop requests all flow through `dispatchNewtabFeaturedBackgroundModalOpenChange` and the controller's close request action.
- `newtab-featured-background-modal-store.ts` no longer exposes `onModalPointerDownCapture` or `dispatchNewtabFeaturedBackgroundModalPointerDownCapture`.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer registers `handleFeaturedBackgroundModalPointerDown`, and the old `target.closest('#background-featured-modal-close')` compatibility path is gone.
- At the time, the essential `.featured-wallpaper-modal` overlay and `.featured-wallpaper-panel` layout rules were moved into `newtab.css`, the actual newtab entry CSS, so the React-owned backdrop target covered the full viewport without relying on deferred CSS. These layout rules were later moved into `FeaturedBackgroundModal.tsx` during the 2026-06-12 Newtab Featured Modal Overlay Tailwind Migration.

#### Newtab React-Owned Document Keyboard Slice

#### Newtab React-Owned Window Pointer Slice

- `NewtabApp` now owns top-level `window.pointermove`, `window.pointerup`, and `window.pointercancel` listeners through a React effect.

#### Newtab React-Owned Lifecycle Event Slice

Completed after the window pointer slice:

- `NewtabApp` now owns top-level `document.visibilitychange` and `window.pagehide` listeners through a React effect.
- `newtab-lifecycle-store.ts` dispatches lifecycle events to the controller's registered lifecycle actions.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer installs `document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', ...)` or `window.addEventListener('pagehide', ...)` from `startNewTabController()`.
- Refresh and cleanup business behavior stays in the controller for now: visible-page refresh can hydrate featured background state, and pagehide still tears down controller timers/caches/actions.

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was read before implementation and verification. No specific Base UI primitive page was required because this slice only moved listener ownership into the React app lifecycle.

- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- Residual searches confirm no controller-owned `document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', ...)` or `window.addEventListener('pagehide', ...)` path remains in `src/newtab`.
- A targeted production-build Playwright newtab lifecycle smoke loaded the built extension in a temporary Chromium profile, created temporary bookmarks/settings, confirmed `visibilitychange` dispatch produces no errors, confirmed the add-bookmark menu path remains usable after `visibilitychange`, confirmed `pagehide` cleanup dispatch produces no errors, confirmed reload after pagehide restores search and Speed Dial interactions, reported no console/page errors, and confirmed no `data-react-island-host` nodes render.

#### Newtab React-Owned Dashboard Route Message Slice

Completed after the lifecycle event slice:

- `NewtabApp` now owns top-level `window.hashchange` and `window.message` listeners through a React effect.
- `newtab-window-store.ts` dispatches hash and message events to the controller's registered window actions.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer installs top-level `window.addEventListener('hashchange', ...)` or `window.addEventListener('message', ...)` listeners from `bindEvents()`.
- Dashboard route and iframe-message business behavior stays in the controller for now: `#dashboard` opens the overlay, the iframe ready message marks the overlay ready, and the iframe close message closes the overlay and clears the route.

#### Newtab React-Owned Node Change Event Slice

Completed after the dashboard route/message slice:

- `NewtabApp` now owns the `newtab:content-layout-nodes-change`, `newtab:dashboard-overlay-nodes-change`, and `newtab:settings-drawer-nodes-change` custom event listeners through a React effect.
- `newtab-window-store.ts` dispatches these React-ref node-change events to the controller's registered window actions.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer installs these custom event listeners from `bindEvents()`.
- Node-change business behavior stays in the controller for now: content layout changes schedule adaptive layout, settings drawer node changes initialize drawer integration, and dashboard overlay node changes initialize/render dashboard integration.

#### Newtab React-Owned Chrome Bookmark Event Slice

Completed after the React-owned node-change event slice:

- Added `newtab-bookmark-events-store.ts` as the narrow dispatch bridge between React-owned Chrome bookmark events and controller bookmark refresh actions.
- `NewtabApp` now owns `chrome.bookmarks.onCreated`, `onRemoved`, `onChanged`, and `onMoved` listener registration and cleanup in the same React effect that owns the top-level newtab browser lifecycle listeners.
- `newtab-controller.ts` registers bookmark event actions with `registerNewtabBookmarkEventActions`.
- `newtab-controller.ts` no longer installs direct `chrome.bookmarks?.onCreated/onRemoved/onChanged/onMoved.addListener(...)` handlers from `bindEvents()`.
- Bookmark business behavior stays in the controller for now: incremental create/update/remove/move handling, search index invalidation, Speed Dial refresh, dashboard state messages, and fallback debounced refresh scheduling.

## Export Classification

### Popup

- `PopupApp` is the native React entry point, and the old `popup-runtime.ts` / `PopupRuntimeIslands.tsx` bridge is gone.
- `usePopupController` still owns page startup, state initialization, and view-model dispatching; React now owns the top-level browser listener lifecycle.
- Remaining DOM operations are narrow: React root lookup, clipboard fallback textarea creation, active element focus fallback, and legitimate React ref-driven focus/scroll behavior.
- Final destination: smaller popup hooks/services where controller state transitions are non-DOM and React components own focus, scrolling, and layout refs.

#### Popup Ref Cleanup Slice

Completed after the AI provider slice:

- `PopupContentHost` now preserves scroll and reveals the active search result through React refs instead of querying `[data-popup-main-list]` and `[data-result-index].active`.
- `PopupContent` accepts the main list ref and active result ref, and passes the clicked action button element through the React action path.
- `PopupModalsHost` focuses move, smart-folder, AI-provider, edit, and delete modal controls through refs instead of `document.getElementById`.
- `popup-controller.ts` receives the clicked action button as `returnFocusElement` for edit/move/delete dialogs, removing the old `[data-bookmark-id]` selector while preserving close-and-return-focus behavior.

Validation after this slice:

- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warnings about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import and large chunks.
- `git diff --check` reports only CRLF working copy warnings.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- A Playwright popup smoke with a minimal Chrome extension API stub mounted `#popup-app-shell`, focused and typed into `#search-input`, rendered bookmark rows, kept the modal stack attached, and reported 0 warnings.

#### Popup Smart Overlay Shell Slice

Completed after the popup ref cleanup:

- `PopupShell` now subscribes to the smart classifier view and applies `smart-active` to the React-owned `#popup-app-shell`.
- `PopupSmartClassifierHost` no longer mutates `document.body.classList`.
- `popup.css` smart overlay rules now target `.app-shell.smart-active` instead of `body.smart-active`, keeping the layout state inside the React tree.

Validation after this slice:

- `npm run typecheck` passes.

#### Popup Browser Event Ownership Slice

Completed after the popup smart overlay shell slice:

- Added `popup-browser-events-store.ts` as the narrow dispatch bridge between React-owned browser events and popup controller actions.
- `PopupShell` now owns `document.keydown`, `window.pagehide`, and `chrome.storage.onChanged` listener registration and cleanup in a React effect.
- `popup-controller.ts` registers browser event actions with `registerPopupBrowserEventActions`.
- Removed the old controller-owned `AbortController` event binding path for popup `keydown` and `pagehide`.
- Removed direct controller-owned `chrome.storage.onChanged.addListener/removeListener` wiring for auto-analyze status updates.
- Keyboard, cleanup, command-intent, and auto-analyze storage business behavior stays in the controller for now.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was read before implementation and verification. No specific Base UI primitive page was required because this slice only moved browser event listener ownership into the React app lifecycle.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- Residual searches confirm no controller-owned popup `window.addEventListener('pagehide', ...)`, `document.addEventListener('keydown', ...)`, `chrome.storage?.onChanged?.addListener(...)`, `popupControllerEventController`, or old bookmark row query focus fallback remains in `src/popup`.
- A targeted production-build Playwright popup smoke loaded the built extension in a temporary Chromium profile, created temporary bookmarks, confirmed `/` focuses search, ArrowDown moves the active result, Escape clears search, `chrome.storage.local.set` updates the auto-analyze status through the React-owned storage listener, reported no console/page errors, and confirmed no `data-react-island-host` nodes render.

### Newtab

- `NewtabApp` is the native React entry point, and the old `newtab-runtime.ts` / `RuntimeIslands.tsx` bridge is gone.
- `useNewtabController` still owns page startup, settings, background, search, drag, dashboard overlay, and a large amount of imperative DOM coordination.
- Final destination: focused hooks/services for bookmarks, settings, background, search, drag, clock, and dashboard overlay, with React components owning layout classes, refs, and controls.

#### Newtab App Chrome Class Slice

Completed after the popup smart overlay slice:

- `useNewtabAppChromeAttributes` derives non-drag app chrome classes from React stores and applies them to the React-owned `.newtab-app` wrapper.
- `settings-open`, `background-mask-enabled`, `folder-names-hidden`, and `settings-trigger-auto-hide` are no longer written to `document.body`.
- The matching CSS now targets `.newtab-app.*` selectors.
- `NewtabBodyClassesHost` still owns drag-related body classes because drag ghosts are currently mounted under `document.body`; that remains a dedicated drag migration slice.

Validation after this slice:

- `npm run typecheck` passes.

### Options

- `OptionsApp` is the native React entry point, and the old `options-runtime.ts` / options island bridges are gone.
- `useOptionsController` still owns page startup, settings, management workflows, modal state, and several controller business-event handlers; React now owns section chrome, overview state, and Chrome bookmark event listener lifecycle.
- Final destination: `OptionsApp`, options domain controllers, React route/hash state, and non-DOM services.

#### Options Chrome Bookmark Event Ownership Slice

Completed after the popup browser event ownership slice:

- Added `options-bookmark-events-store.ts` as the narrow dispatch bridge between React-owned Chrome bookmark events and options controller refresh actions.
- `OptionsApp` now owns `chrome.bookmarks.onCreated`, `onRemoved`, `onChanged`, `onMoved`, `onChildrenReordered`, and `onImportEnded` listener registration and cleanup in a React effect.
- `options-controller.ts` registers bookmark event actions with `registerOptionsBookmarkEventActions`.
- Removed the old controller-owned `bindBookmarkChangeListeners()` direct Chrome event registration.
- Options catalog refresh behavior stays in the controller for now: debounced `hydrateAvailabilityCatalog({ preserveResults: true })`, dashboard refresh if visible, and warning logging on refresh failure.

- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.

- `OptionsApp` now owns `hashchange`, `popstate`, `curator:options-section-change`, `curator:dashboard-view-ready`, and `message` listener registration and cleanup in a React effect.
- `options-controller.ts` registers window event actions with `registerOptionsWindowEventActions`.
- Removed the old controller-owned direct listener installation for options section route changes, dashboard-view-ready notifications, and newtab-dashboard iframe messages.
- Route and embed business behavior stays in the controller for now: section sync, scroll reset, dashboard entry/exit preparation, newtab dashboard ready messages, and speed-dial state messages.

#### Options Document Event Ownership Slice

Completed after the options top-level window event ownership slice:

- Added `options-document-events-store.ts` as the narrow dispatch bridge between React-owned document events and options controller dashboard/global actions.
- `OptionsApp` now owns `document.pointermove`, `pointerup`, `pointercancel`, `click`, `focusin`, and `keydown` listener registration and cleanup in a React effect.

- Removed the old controller-owned direct document listener installation from `bindEvents()`.

#### Options Custom Action Event Ownership Slice

Completed after the options document event ownership slice:

- Added `options-action-events-store.ts` as the narrow dispatch bridge between React-owned custom `window` action-event listeners and options controller handlers.
- `OptionsApp` now owns registration and cleanup for dashboard, scope picker, availability, AI analysis, backup, tag management, model selector, provider settings, shortcuts, feature settings, content snapshot, pagination, history, redirects, duplicates, folder cleanup, ignore rules, recycle, managed modal, and folder picker action events through `OPTIONS_ACTION_EVENT_BINDINGS`.
- `options-controller.ts` registers all existing action handlers with `registerOptionsActionEventActions`.
- Removed the old controller-owned `bindEvents()` custom `window.addEventListener(...)` installation path.
- All action business behavior stays in the controller for now; this slice only moves listener lifecycle ownership into React.

#### Options AI Provider Anchor Attention Slice

Completed after the options custom action event ownership slice:

- `useOptionsSectionChrome` now publishes the route anchor and a route revision alongside the section key.
- `OptionsApp` owns the AI provider anchor ref and the `.options-main` scroll container ref.
- Opening `#general:ai-provider` now scrolls through React-owned refs and increments an attention request id passed into `AiProviderSettings`.
- `AiProviderSettings` derives the temporary `.attention` class from that React prop instead of listening for a custom window attention event.
- `options-controller.ts` no longer queries `document.getElementById('ai-provider-settings')`, calls `scrollToSectionAnchor()`, or dispatches a provider settings `attention` action.

#### Options Route Scroll Reset Slice

Completed after the options AI provider anchor attention slice:

- `OptionsApp` now owns `history.scrollRestoration = 'manual'` while the React options app is mounted, and restores the previous value on unmount.
- `OptionsApp` owns top-reset behavior for route changes without anchors through React refs to `.options-shell` and `.options-main`.
- `options-controller.ts` no longer calls `resetOptionsScrollPosition()` and no longer writes `window.scrollTo`, `document.documentElement.scrollTop`, or `document.body.scrollTop`.
- Anchor routes such as `#general:ai-provider` continue to bypass the generic top reset and use the React-owned anchor attention path.

#### Options Tag Management Controls Store Cleanup

Completed after the AI config link store cleanup:

- Removed the short-lived fill/form event exports from `tag-management-events.ts`; only the user action event remains.

#### Dashboard Search Help Popover Store Cleanup

Completed after the tag management controls store cleanup:

- `DashboardPanel` no longer installs `dashboard:search-help-open`, `dashboard:search-help-close`, or `dashboard:search-help-toggle` window event listeners.
- `dashboard-view-store.ts` now keeps `searchControls.searchHelpOpen`, and `DashboardPanel` controls the Base UI Popover with `patchDashboardSearchControlsState`.
- `dashboard-controller.ts` preserves the open state while publishing search controls, so search rerenders do not collapse the help popover.
- Removed the legacy `toggle-search-help` dashboard action detail and the final CSS rule that hid `#dashboard-search-help-toggle`.
- Updated the shared Popover wrappers so a provided `triggerId` is passed to `Popover.Trigger` as its DOM id, matching Base UI controlled-trigger semantics and preserving stable smoke/test anchors.
- `DashboardSearchHelpButton` now follows Base UI composition requirements by accepting `ref` and spreading injected trigger props onto its native button.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover.md`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/handbook/composition.md` were read before implementation and verification.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- `npm run build` passes with the known `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import warning.
- Residual searches confirm `dashboard:search-help` and `toggle-search-help` no longer appear in `src/options`.
- A targeted production-build Playwright dashboard search-help smoke loaded the built extension in a temporary Chromium profile, created temporary bookmarks, confirmed `#dashboard-search-help-toggle` is visible with a stable id, clicked it to open `#dashboard-search-help-popover`, confirmed the trigger receives `aria-expanded="true"` and `data-popup-open`, closed with Escape, confirmed focus returns to the trigger, and confirmed no console/page errors or `data-react-island-host` nodes render.

### Dashboard

Dashboard runtime exports fall into three groups:

- Pure or mostly pure helpers to extract early:
  - drag snapshot helpers
  - selection label helpers
  - virtual metrics helpers
  - speed dial message helpers
  - favicon fallback helper
- React controller actions:
  - search/filter input handling
  - selection mutations
  - tag popover/editor state
  - drag state
  - move/delete workflows
- Imperative UI render and DOM event handlers to replace:
  - `renderDashboardSection`
  - dashboard card/list rendering
  - virtual list DOM mutation
  - tag editor/popover DOM positioning
  - remaining drag hit detection and move/delete workflow coordination

### Tag Cloud Runtime

Completed during the options tag management slice:

- `src/options/sections/tag-cloud-runtime.ts` was moved into `src/options/components/useTagCloudPhysics.ts`.
- `TagManagementCloud` owns the cloud root ref and starts/stops the physics loop with a React layout effect.
- `tag-management.ts` no longer starts physics with a returned DOM node; direct component unmount cleanup stops pointer listeners, ResizeObserver, RAF, drag classes, click suppression, and direct transform updates.
- The physics hook still mutates transforms for animation, but that imperative work is scoped behind a component ref and effect cleanup instead of an external section runtime.

### 2026-06-09 Options Direct-Render Cleanup

Completed after the AI results pagination and settings-panel direct-render slices:

- Backup controls:
  - Added `BackupControls`, `backup-controls-store.ts`, and `backup-controls-types.ts`.
  - `BackupPanel` now renders backup/tag-data controls directly instead of an empty `#backup-controls` host.
  - `options-controller.ts` now publishes backup/tag-data state through `publishBackupControls`.
  - Deleted `BackupPreviewIsland.tsx`.
  - `shared-options/dom.ts` no longer caches `dom.backupControls`.
- Tag management controls:
  - Added `TagManagementControls`, `tag-management-controls-store.ts`, and `tag-management-controls-types.ts`.
  - `TagManagementPanel` now renders the controls directly.
  - `tag-management.ts` publishes summary/loading/status state through `publishTagManagementControls`.
  - Deleted `TagManagementControlsIsland.tsx`.
  - `shared-options/dom.ts` no longer caches `dom.tagManagementControls`.
- Shared non-AI pagination:
  - Added `ResultsPagination`, `results-pagination-store.ts`, `results-pagination-events.ts`, and `results-pagination-types.ts`.
  - Availability review, availability failed, and redirects pagination now render as React components in `CorePanels.tsx` / `TaskPanels.tsx`.
  - `options-controller.ts` publishes availability pagination state and handles typed `options:results-pagination` events.
  - `redirects.ts` publishes redirect pagination state through the same store.
  - Deleted `ResultsPaginationIsland.tsx` and removed the old delegated `data-results-page` click handler.
  - `shared-options/dom.ts` no longer caches `dom.availabilityReviewPagination`, `dom.availabilityFailedPagination`, or `dom.redirectPagination`.
- Availability loading labels:
  - Added `LoadingLabel`, `loading-label-store.ts`, and `loading-label-types.ts`.
  - Availability primary action, stop action, and progress label now render React children directly and subscribe to published label state.
  - Deleted `LoadingLabelIsland.tsx` and removed temporary `data-react-island-host` creation from `options-controller.ts`.
- Redirect controls:
  - Added `RedirectControls`, `redirect-controls-store.ts`, and `redirect-controls-types.ts`.
  - `RedirectsPanel` renders controls directly; `redirects.ts` publishes count/selection/subtitle state.
  - Deleted `RedirectControlsIsland.tsx`.
  - `shared-options/dom.ts` no longer caches `dom.redirectControls`.
- Recycle controls:
  - Added `RecycleControls`, `recycle-controls-store.ts`, and `recycle-controls-types.ts`.
  - `RecyclePanel` renders controls directly; `recycle.ts` publishes count/selection/busy state.
  - Deleted `RecycleControlsIsland.tsx`.
  - `shared-options/dom.ts` no longer caches `dom.recycleControls`.

### 2026-06-09 Availability And History Scope Trigger Direct Render

Completed after the managed modal roving keyboard events slice:

- `options-controller.ts` publishes availability/history scope trigger label, copy, title, and disabled state with `publishScopePickerTrigger`.

### 2026-06-09 Managed Modal Open And Focus Direct Render

Completed after the folder picker roving focus direct-render slice:

- `options-modals-types.ts` and `options-modals-store.ts` now carry `open` state for delete, confirm, move, and scope dialogs.
- Move/scope modal state also carries `finalFocusId` so React can return focus to the correct availability/history/AI trigger or move action after close.
- `OptionsModals` now drives Base UI `Dialog.Root` with React store state instead of listening for the old `options:managed-modal-state` event.
- `OptionsModals` now passes function-valued `initialFocus` and `finalFocus` to the Base UI Dialog popup so search inputs, cancel buttons, and return focus are owned by React/Base UI.
- `FolderPickerResults` receives the search input refs directly and handles Escape-to-search locally instead of asking the controller to focus DOM nodes.
- Removed controller-owned `setManagedModalHidden`, `focusModal`, `trapModalFocus`, modal focusable queries, modal return-focus bookkeeping, and the old `options:managed-modal-state` event bridge.

### 2026-06-10 Newtab Base UI Focus Trap Removal

Completed after the newtab component-level focus trap audit:

- Removed the dashboard overlay's component-scoped `document.addEventListener('keydown')` Tab wrapping and its local `querySelectorAll` focusable scan.
- Removed the featured background dialog's component-scoped `document.addEventListener('keydown')` Tab wrapping and its local `querySelectorAll` focusable scan.
- Removed the settings drawer's component-scoped `document.addEventListener('keydown')` Tab wrapping.
- Let Base UI Dialog/Drawer popup focus containment own the active focus loop instead of duplicating it in component code.
- Changed the settings drawer root from `modal={false}` to `modal="trap-focus"` so the controlled Base UI Drawer replaces the previous manual Tab trap without adding pointer-inert behavior.
- Follow-up smoke found the featured background dialog returned focus to `body` after Escape because it is controlled from an external React button rather than a colocated `Dialog.Trigger`; `FeaturedBackgroundModal` now keeps Base UI `finalFocus`, releases the Dialog modal trap during the closing phase, and falls back to focusing the React-published `#background-featured-picker` trigger without reintroducing a Tab trap.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/dialog.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/drawer.md` were read before implementation.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warning about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import.
- Residual searches confirm the three component-scoped Tab trap listeners no longer remain in `DashboardOverlay.tsx`, `FeaturedBackgroundModal.tsx`, or `SettingsDrawer.tsx`; at this point the remaining settings drawer focusable-query helper was used only for explicit section/first-control focus requests, and a later 2026-06-11 follow-up removed that helper too.
- A source scan confirms the only remaining newtab `document.addEventListener('keydown')` is the top-level React app lifecycle listener in `NewtabApp.tsx`.
- A production-extension Playwright smoke against `dist` confirmed no `data-react-island-host` nodes, settings drawer Tab focus remains inside `#newtab-settings-drawer`, the Appearance tab remains clickable with Drawer `modal="trap-focus"`, the featured background modal opens and keeps Tab focus inside `#background-featured-modal`, Escape returns focus to `#background-featured-picker`, Escape closes the drawer and returns focus to `#newtab-settings-trigger`, and the dashboard overlay keeps Tab focus inside `#newtab-dashboard-overlay` before Escape close. The only request failures were existing missing bundled `featured-gallery-thumbnails/*` fallbacks and remote gallery images aborted when the smoke closed the page.

### 2026-06-10 Newtab Instant Wallpaper State Host

Completed after the focus-trap slice:

- Added `newtab-instant-wallpaper-store.ts` to preserve the startup script's current `<html>` instant wallpaper state and expose later wallpaper state updates through `useSyncExternalStore`.
- Added `NewtabInstantWallpaperHost`, mounted from `NewtabShell`, to initially own post-mount synchronization of `<html>` `loading-wallpaper`, `newtab-booting`, `instant-wallpaper-ready`, `instant-wallpaper-remote-ready`, `data-instant-wallpaper-*`, and instant wallpaper CSS variables. A later follow-up moved the runtime state from `<html>` mutation to React-owned `.newtab-app` attributes/styles, leaving the host as first-paint startup cleanup only.
- Updated `newtab-controller.ts` wallpaper helpers so `markWallpaperReady`, `markWallpaperPending`, `markInstantWallpaperRemoteReady`, `ensureInstantWallpaperFallbackStyles`, `clearInstantWallpaperFallbackStyles`, placeholder color updates, and featured display CSS updates publish React state instead of directly mutating `document.documentElement`.
- Kept `public/instant-wallpaper-boot.js` and the inline startup style as the pre-React first-paint path; the React host removes `#instant-wallpaper-startup-style` after it takes ownership.

A later follow-up removed those runtime `<html>` writes from the host.

### 2026-06-10 Newtab Bookmark Menu Position State

Completed after the instant wallpaper state host slice:

- `BookmarkMenusHost` now computes edit/add menu viewport clamping through `useBookmarkMenuPosition` and renders the final `left`/`top` values through React `style` props.
- Removed the old `positionMenu` helper's direct `menu.style.left` and `menu.style.top` writes.
- Added `ResizeObserver` and window resize updates so menu position remains clamped when menu content or viewport dimensions change.
- Clamping now uses the tightest available layout viewport from `window.innerWidth`/`innerHeight`, `visualViewport`, and `documentElement.clientWidth`/`clientHeight`, with a 16 px safety margin so fixed-position menus stay visibly inside extension page bounds during entry animation.
- Kept focus ownership in `useBookmarkMenuLayout`, now separated from positioning so the hook only handles focused action / first input behavior.
- Cleaned `useBookmarkActionRefs` lazy initialization after React Doctor flagged eager `Map` construction.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was read before implementation; no specific Base UI primitive was changed.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warning about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import.
- Residual search confirms no `menu.style`, `.style.left`, `.style.top`, or old `positionMenu` helper remains in `src/newtab/components/BookmarkMenusHost.tsx`.
- A production-extension Playwright smoke loaded `dist` in a temporary Chromium profile, created a temporary source folder/bookmark/workspace, confirmed right-clicking a bookmark opens `.bookmark-edit-menu` inside the viewport with React-owned `left`/`top`, confirmed right-clicking the bottom-right blank area opens `.bookmark-add-menu` inside the viewport with React-owned `left`/`top`, and confirmed no `data-react-island-host` nodes render.

### 2026-06-10 Options Tag Cloud Physics Event State

Completed after the newtab bookmark menu position slice:

- `TagManagementCloud` now owns tag-word refs and passes React pointer/click handlers into `useTagCloudPhysics`.
- `useTagCloudPhysics` now builds particles from React `TagCloudItem` data and React-published refs instead of querying `[data-tag-cloud-word]` nodes.
- Dragging visual state is rendered through React class names instead of `element.classList.add/remove('is-dragging')`.
- Drag-click suppression is tracked in hook state refs and consumed by the React `onClick` path instead of writing `data-tag-drag-suppress-until`.
- Static tag cloud item data now flows through typed item props instead of per-word `data-tag-*` attributes.
- The remaining direct `element.style.transform` write is intentionally retained inside the physics animation loop because it is a high-frequency animation/media-style ref effect, not ordinary UI rendering.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was read before implementation; no specific Base UI primitive was changed.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warning about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import.
- Residual search confirms no `querySelector`, `closest`, `classList`, `dataset`, `data-tag-cloud-word`, `data-tag-*` item attributes, old root event listeners, or `tagDragSuppressUntil` paths remain in `TagManagementCloud.tsx` / `useTagCloudPhysics.ts`.
- A production-extension Playwright smoke loaded `dist` in a temporary Chromium profile, created temporary bookmarks and a local tag index, confirmed the tag cloud renders without old `data-tag-*` markers, clicked `React` and verified the source tag input plus target-input focus update, dragged the React tag and verified React-owned `.is-dragging` appears then clears, confirmed physics transforms apply, and confirmed no `data-react-island-host` nodes render.

- Kept active React/Base UI styling for current options sections, dashboard results, modal folder pickers, AI provider controls, availability controls, and shared `detect-results-*` result surfaces.
- Follow-up cleanup removed the obsolete `.dashboard-card-region.is-selection-motion-shifting` and `.dashboard-card-region.is-selection-motion-settling` rules after confirming the selection-motion class writers were already deleted and the active dashboard panel now publishes `data-dashboard-selection-motion`.
- The remaining options CSS unused-class scanner hits are `.dot-matrix-loader--spiral` / `.dot-matrix-loader--bar`, which are produced dynamically by `DotMatrixLoader`, plus `is-core`, `is-body`, and `is-mist`, which are produced dynamically by `TagManagementCloud` from `is-${item.tier}`.

### 2026-06-11 Newtab Instant Wallpaper App-State Ownership

Completed after the popup CSS token/reset consolidation:

- `useNewtabAppChromeAttributes` now consumes `useNewtabInstantWallpaperView` and renders runtime wallpaper loading/ready classes, `data-instant-wallpaper-*` attributes, and instant wallpaper CSS variables on the React-owned `.newtab-app` root.
- `src/newtab/newtab.css` now scopes runtime wallpaper preview, loader, remote-ready, and background-image selectors to `.newtab-app` instead of `html` / `body`, with `.newtab-app` acting as an explicit isolated stacking context.
- `NewtabInstantWallpaperHost` no longer mirrors runtime wallpaper state to `document.documentElement`; it only removes the pre-React startup `<html>` classes/data/style variables and `#instant-wallpaper-startup-style` once React has taken ownership.
- The pre-React startup HTML/script remains the first-paint bootstrap path. It now publishes an explicit `__CURATOR_INSTANT_WALLPAPER_BOOT_VIEW__` snapshot for React, so `newtab-instant-wallpaper-store.ts` no longer scrapes initial app state from `<html>` classes, dataset, or inline style variables.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` was re-read before/for the slice. No Base UI primitive composition changed.
- Residual search confirms `src/newtab/newtab.css` no longer contains `html.loading-wallpaper`, `html.instant-wallpaper-*`, or `body::before` runtime wallpaper selectors, and `NewtabInstantWallpaperHost` no longer uses `classList.toggle` or `style.setProperty`.

### 2026-06-11 Newtab Instant Wallpaper Boot Snapshot Handoff

Completed after the dashboard panel event bridge cleanup:

- `public/instant-wallpaper-boot.js` now keeps its first-paint `<html>` mutations as visual bootstrap only and mirrors the same loading/ready/image/preview/signature values into a small `__CURATOR_INSTANT_WALLPAPER_BOOT_VIEW__` object on `globalThis`.
- `newtab-instant-wallpaper-store.ts` consumes and clears that boot-view snapshot during module initialization, normalizes malformed values back to the neutral wallpaper view, and no longer reads `document.documentElement`, `classList`, `dataset`, or inline style properties to build React state.
- The boot script stops republishing the global snapshot after React marks it consumed, preventing late image probes or loader timers from recreating stale startup state after the React store has taken ownership.
- `NewtabInstantWallpaperHost` remains the one-way cleanup boundary that removes the startup `<html>` classes/data/style variables and startup style tag after React owns the wallpaper state on `.newtab-app`.

Validation after this slice:

- No Base UI primitive composition changed; the slice only narrowed the first-paint bootstrap handoff.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warning about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- Targeted source search confirms `src/newtab/newtab-instant-wallpaper-store.ts` has no `document.documentElement`, `classList.contains`, `dataset.instantWallpaper`, or `style.getPropertyValue` reads.
- A targeted production-build Playwright newtab smoke with Chrome API, bookmark tree, storage, permission, tab, and startup `localStorage` stubs confirmed the boot script publishes a wallpaper signature/image/preview snapshot, React consumes the boot-view global into `.newtab-app`, the consumed sentinel is set, `#instant-wallpaper-startup-style` is removed, startup `<html>` classes/data are cleared, and there are no actionable page errors.

### 2026-06-12 Newtab Bookmark Menu Viewport Cleanup

Completed after the newtab viewport measurement cleanup:

- Removed `BookmarkMenusHost.tsx`'s remaining `document.documentElement` viewport-size read from bookmark edit/add menu clamping.
- `getMenuViewportSize` now uses the window and `visualViewport` dimensions only, keeping the viewport boundary at the React host layer rather than reading the document root.
- `getClampedMenuPosition` now clamps with the menu element's untransformed layout dimensions (`offsetWidth`/`offsetHeight`) before falling back to `getBoundingClientRect()`, so the fixed-position menu stays inside the 16 px safety margin even while the enter animation is transform-scaling the surface.
- The menu's actual size measurement remains a legitimate React-ref element measurement.

### 2026-06-12 Shared Motion Duration Cleanup

Completed after the newtab bookmark menu viewport cleanup:

- Removed `src/shared/motion.ts`'s root CSS variable lookup for modal close timing.
- Deleted the now-unused CSS time parser and root-duration helper exports from `src/shared/motion.ts`.
- `getModalCloseDurationMs` now returns the shared `150ms` close duration directly, or `1ms` when `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` matches, aligning with the existing `--modal-close-dur` tokens without reading `document.documentElement` or computed styles at runtime.
- Popup modal presence and newtab featured-background modal close timers continue to call `getModalCloseDurationMs`, preserving their current timing contract.

### 2026-06-12 Base UI Hidden Input Attribute Cleanup

Completed after the newtab bookmark drag ghost style cleanup:

- Removed the wrapper-level `inputAttributes` and `inputClassName` mutation effects from `src/ui/base/Checkbox.tsx`, `Switch.tsx`, `Select.tsx`, and `Slider.tsx`.
- Kept Base UI's documented hidden/nested input refs and the existing `syncInputState` compatibility bridge, but stopped applying custom classes or arbitrary attributes to those inputs with `setAttribute`, `removeAttribute`, and direct `input.className` writes.
- Moved migrated settings drawer hooks to React-owned visible controls: module and search-engine data attributes now pass through `SwitchControl` to the visible Base UI switch root, and select accessible names now use the wrapper's `ariaLabel` prop on the visible `Select.Trigger`.
- Kept slider accessible names on Base UI's documented `Slider.Thumb getAriaLabel` path, which Base UI forwards to the nested range input, without custom attribute mutation.
- Consolidated `SliderControl`'s related uncontrolled compatibility state into a small reducer so the cleanup does not introduce React Doctor related-state warnings.
- Updated the bookmark edit menu icon mode select to use the same declarative select trigger label path.

Validation after this slice:

- `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/checkbox.md`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/switch.md`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/select.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/slider.md` were re-read for the slice. Installed Base UI type definitions and implementation files were also checked; the docs/types expose hidden/nested input access through `inputRef`, not a declarative hidden-input prop slot.
- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warning about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import.
- `npx react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports `100 / 100`.
- Residual targeted search confirms `src/ui/base/Checkbox.tsx`, `Switch.tsx`, `Select.tsx`, and `Slider.tsx` no longer contain wrapper-level `setAttribute`, `removeAttribute`, `input.className`, or hidden-input `inputAttributes`/`inputClassName` forwarding. `NumberInput.tsx` still has an unrelated `inputClassName` prop for a React-owned visual stepper span.
