Controller behavior publishes `DashboardDragOverlayState.closing`, `visible`, `moving`, `deleteTargetActive`, folder hover state, preview view model, and preview transform through the React dashboard view store; behavior still depends on stable markers such as `[data-dashboard-card]`, `data-dashboard-bookmark-id`, `data-dashboard-no-drag`, `role="option"`, `data-dashboard-delete-active`, and `data-dashboard-moving`, not the retired closing marker.
- Added direct React-owned close-state class constants for the overlay, delete target, drop panel, and preview: `DASHBOARD_DRAG_OVERLAY_CLOSING_STATE_CLASS`, `DASHBOARD_DELETE_DROP_TARGET_CLOSING_STATE_CLASS`, `DASHBOARD_DROP_PANEL_CLOSING_STATE_CLASS`, and `DASHBOARD_DRAG_PREVIEW_CLOSING_STATE_CLASS`.
- Removed rendered `data-dashboard-closing` from `#dashboard-drag-overlay`, the delete drop target, the folder drop panel, and the drag preview. `DashboardDragOverlay` now applies the close-state classes directly from `state.closing`, preserving overlay backdrop exit animation, pointer suppression, delete target opacity/transform/transition, drop panel opacity/transform/easing, preview opacity/easing, `aria-hidden`, folder drop grid behavior, hover event handlers, and controller-published preview transforms.
- Kept `data-dashboard-delete-active` and `data-dashboard-moving` intentionally because they are separate drag/drop state markers still used by Tailwind selectors and smoke-covered behavior. During validation, the first drag smoke found that plain `opacity-0` closing classes lost to base `opacity-100` on the same element; the fix changed the drop-panel and preview closing utilities to important variants so the direct state classes preserve the previous selector specificity.
- No Base UI primitive API or composition changed in this slice; the drag overlay is a React-rendered drag/drop surface.
- No `src/options/options.css` lines changed by this slice; the current file is 61 physical lines before and after validation. Retired token/dependency: `data-dashboard-closing` / `[data-dashboard-closing=true]`.

Validation after this slice:

- `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `npm run build` passes with the pre-existing Vite warning about `src/popup/search-lookup.ts` mixed dynamic/static import.
- A targeted production-build Playwright smoke served `dist` from a stdin Node script, injected Chrome bookmark/storage/permission/runtime/tab/session/notification/command/download stubs before React boot, used the installed Chrome executable, opened desktop Dashboard with a three-card fixture plus one folder target, started a real pointer drag from the card title, and confirmed the overlay opens with zero `[data-dashboard-closing]` markers, `aria-hidden="false"`, overlay `pointer-events: auto`, `animation-name: options-backdrop-enter`, drop panel `opacity: 1`, drag preview `opacity: 1`, a live preview transform, and zero legacy drag/island nodes.
- The same smoke pressed Escape and confirmed close-time visual state is now class-owned with zero `[data-dashboard-closing]` markers: overlay `pointer-events: none`, `animation-name: options-backdrop-exit`, cubic-bezier backdrop timing, delete target `opacity: 0`, delete target close transform, drop panel `opacity: 0`, drop panel close transform and cubic-bezier timing, preview `opacity: 0`, and preview cubic-bezier timing. It also confirmed the overlay returns to `aria-hidden="true"`, no page errors occur, no legacy `.dashboard-drag-preview`, `#dashboard-drag-card-preview`, or `data-react-island-host` nodes render, and no `chrome.bookmarks.move`, `chrome.bookmarks.remove`, or `chrome.bookmarks.removeTree` calls occur.
- `C:\nodejs\npx.ps1 react-doctor@latest --verbose --scope changed` reports no issues and `100 / 100`.
- `C:\nodejs\npx.ps1 react-doctor@latest --verbose --diff` reports no issues and `100 / 100`, with the tool's deprecation notice for `--diff`.
- `git diff --check -- src/options/components/DashboardPanel.tsx src/options/components/dashboard-classes.ts docs/react-migration-phase-0-inventory.md src/options/options.css` reports only known LF-to-CRLF warnings. Because `DashboardView.tsx` is currently untracked in this working tree, `git diff --check --no-index -- NUL src\options\components\DashboardView.tsx` was also run and reported only the known LF-to-CRLF warning; a trailing-whitespace scan reported no matches.
- Targeted residual scans confirm no `data-dashboard-closing`, `[data-dashboard-closing`, `data-dashboard-tag-editor-closing`, `[data-dashboard-tag-editor-closing`, `dashboard-drag-card-preview`, legacy `dashboard-drag-preview`, or `data-react-island-host` token remains in live options source or current built assets. Positive scans confirm the new drag overlay close-state class constants remain in source, the retained `data-dashboard-delete-active` and `data-dashboard-moving` markers remain intentional, and no temporary Playwright `.mjs`/`.js`/`.ts`/`.cjs` scripts remain under `output/playwright/`.

### 2026-06-18 Dashboard Drag Hover/Moving Marker Retirement

- Re-audited `data-dashboard-delete-active` and `data-dashboard-moving` across live dashboard React source, controller drag/drop flow, options CSS, built assets, and historical inventory notes before editing. No controller selector, ARIA relationship, focus path, or drop business logic depends on those rendered markers; behavior flows through `DashboardDragOverlayState.deleteTargetActive`, `DashboardDragOverlayState.moving`, folder target `active`, typed `drag-hover-delete` / `drag-hover-folder` actions, `role="option"`, and `aria-selected`.
- Re-read `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button` before changing the folder drop target Button styling path. No Base UI primitive API or composition changed; folder drop targets still render through the existing `Button` wrapper with `type="button"`, `role="option"`, and `aria-selected`.
- Removed rendered `data-dashboard-delete-active` and `data-dashboard-moving` from the drag delete target, and removed rendered `data-dashboard-moving` from folder drop target buttons.
- Added direct React-owned state class constants in `src/options/components/dashboard-classes.ts`: `DASHBOARD_FOLDER_DROP_CARD_MOVING_STATE_CLASS`, `DASHBOARD_DELETE_DROP_TARGET_ACTIVE_STATE_CLASS`, `DASHBOARD_DELETE_DROP_TARGET_ACTIVE_TRANSFORM_STATE_CLASS`, and `DASHBOARD_DELETE_DROP_TARGET_MOVING_STATE_CLASS`.
- Updated `DashboardPanel.tsx` and `DashboardView.tsx` to compose those state classes from React state instead of Tailwind attribute selectors. The delete target active transform is intentionally split from the active color/shadow class so closing state can keep the close transform precedence when active and closing overlap.
- Preserved folder hover selection through `aria-selected="true"`, delete/folder hover dispatch, overlay closing classes, drag preview transform publishing, and destructive-operation guards.
- No `src/options/options.css` lines changed by this slice; the current file is 61 physical lines before and after validation. Retired tokens/dependencies: `data-dashboard-delete-active`, `[data-dashboard-delete-active=true]`, `data-dashboard-moving`, and `[data-dashboard-moving=true]`.

### 2026-06-18 Dashboard Ready Marker Retirement

- Added direct React-owned ready-state classes in `src/options/components/dashboard-classes.ts`: `DASHBOARD_PANEL_NOT_READY_STATE_CLASS` for hidden/pointer-disabled dashboard chrome before readiness, and `DASHBOARD_LOADING_SCREEN_READY_STATE_CLASS` for hiding the loading overlay once ready.
- Updated `DashboardPanel.tsx` to compose the not-ready and loading-ready classes from `panelChrome.ready`, preserving the dashboard section id, `data-dashboard-root`, loading overlay aria label, toolbar/search controls, selection bar, result grid, and dashboard card rendering.
- During validation, plain non-important `invisible opacity-0` utilities lost to the base `visible opacity-100` loading/chrome classes on the same elements. The final ready-state classes use important utilities so the direct React-owned state classes preserve the previous marker-selector precedence.

### 2026-06-18 Dashboard Icon Tooltip Marker Retirement

- Re-audited `data-dashboard-tooltip` across live dashboard React source, dashboard controller behavior, options CSS, current built options/dashboard assets, and historical inventory notes before editing. The marker had no controller selector, drag behavior, ARIA dependency, or business-logic dependency; its live purpose was limited to CSS pseudo-element tooltip copy through `attr(data-dashboard-tooltip)`.
- Re-read `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/tooltip.md` before touching Tooltip composition. The docs confirm Tooltip is a styling-agnostic hover/focus hint for sighted users and that triggers should keep their own accessible labels.
- Updated `src/ui/base/Tooltip.tsx` so the app Tooltip wrapper renders the provided React element as the actual Base UI `Tooltip.Trigger`, avoiding a wrapper `span` around buttons or links. Added `TooltipTriggerShell` for cases where another Base UI trigger must be composed as the same underlying trigger element.
- Extended `InlineMenu` with an optional `triggerWrapper` function so dashboard can wrap the generated Base UI `Menu.Trigger` in `TooltipTriggerShell` without adding an extra nested button or weakening menu keyboard behavior.
- Moved dashboard card icon tooltip visuals out of the `after:content-[attr(data-dashboard-tooltip)]` pseudo-element path and into `DASHBOARD_CARD_TOOLTIP_CLASS`, applied to Base UI `Tooltip.Popup`.
- Removed rendered `data-dashboard-tooltip` attributes from the dashboard open-link action, copy action, and card more-menu trigger while preserving `aria-label`, `data-dashboard-no-drag`, link/button semantics, Base UI menu trigger state, hover/focus visual states, copy/open actions, and more-menu keyboard opening.
- No `src/options/options.css` lines changed by this slice; the current file is 73 physical lines before and after validation. Retired token/dependency: `data-dashboard-tooltip` / `attr(data-dashboard-tooltip)`.

- Re-read `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md` before editing because the retired rendered ids were on a local Popover backed by `@base-ui/react/popover` plus a native button trigger render target. - Removed `triggerId="dashboard-search-help-toggle"` from the dashboard search-help Popover, removed the default fallback `id="dashboard-search-help-toggle"` from `DashboardSearchHelpButton`, and removed the popup `id="dashboard-search-help-popover"`, while preserving the controlled `searchHelpOpen` state, inline portal, native button trigger, explicit `type="button"`, accessible name `查看高级搜索语法`, `align="end"`, `sideOffset={8}`, help copy, popup styling, Escape dismissal, and focus return behavior.

Base UI does not emit `aria-controls` in this idless popup path, so this slice treats role/name/focus behavior as the accessibility contract rather than a stable selector relationship.

### 2026-06-20 Options Managed Modal Escape Keydown Retirement

- Re-audited the remaining options document `keydown` bridge before editing instead of assuming the previous stopping point. - Preserved the existing controller modal action reducer: confirm dismiss/cancel resolves `false`, scope dismiss/cancel closes the scope modal, move dismiss/cancel closes the move modal, and delete dismiss/cancel closes the delete modal. No Base UI primitive API or composition changed in this slice.

- Retired tokens/surfaces: the document `keydown` managed modal Escape checks for `confirmModalOpen`, `scopeModalOpen`, `moveModalOpen`, and `deleteModalOpen`, plus the direct Escape calls to `resolveConfirmModal(false)`, `closeScopeModal()`, `closeMoveModal()`, and `closeDeleteModal()` from that document handler.

### 2026-06-20 Dashboard Panel Click/Focus Bridge Retirement

- Re-read `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt`, `https://base-ui.com/react/components/menu.md`, and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover.md` before changing the Dashboard card-menu/tag-popover outside-close path. The slice keeps the existing Base UI Menu and Popover primitive composition unchanged.
- Re-audited the remaining `data-dashboard-no-drag` markers before editing. The card more-menu wrapper and hidden-tag Popover popup still sit inside the card event path as non-native surfaces, so they remain behavior dependencies; this slice left them intact.
- Moved Dashboard outside click/focus cleanup from document-level `click` and `focusin` bridge channels to `#dashboard` panel-local listeners installed by `DashboardPanel` while the panel is visible. The handlers still route to the same controller containment logic, but the event source is now the React-owned Dashboard panel boundary instead of `document`.
- Removed `dispatchOptionsDocumentClick`, `dispatchOptionsDocumentFocusIn`, `onDocumentClick`, and `onDocumentFocusIn` from `options-document-events-store.ts`, removed the corresponding options-controller registration, and renamed the controller/lazy handlers from `handleDashboardDocumentClick` / `handleDashboardDocumentFocusIn` to `handleDashboardPanelClick` / `handleDashboardPanelFocusIn`.
- Preserved the retained document `keydown`, `pointermove`, `pointerup`, and `pointercancel` bridge channels because Dashboard Escape handling and drag continuation still depend on those global routes after React-owned `drag-start-card`.
- No stylesheet rules were deleted in this slice; `src/options/options.css` remains 61 physical lines / 50 non-empty lines.
- Retired tokens/surfaces: the Dashboard document `click`/`focusin` bridge, `dispatchOptionsDocumentClick`, `dispatchOptionsDocumentFocusIn`, `onDocumentClick`, `onDocumentFocusIn`, `handleDashboardDocumentClick`, and `handleDashboardDocumentFocusIn`.

### 2026-06-20 Options Shortcut Action Event Retirement

### 2026-06-20 Dashboard Panel React Event Boundary

- Preserved the same controller containment logic by forwarding `event.nativeEvent` to `runDashboardPanelClick` / `runDashboardPanelFocusIn`; the click-side cleanup now runs on pointer-down capture so outside menu/tag-editor dismissal stays React-owned without adding an interactive click handler to a non-interactive section.
- Left the document `keydown`, `pointermove`, `pointerup`, and `pointercancel` listeners untouched because they still cover Escape route exit and drag continuation after a React-owned card drag starts.
- Existing Base UI composition was left unchanged; this slice only moved the panel-local outside-boundary event source into React event props.
- No stylesheet lines were changed in this slice; current `src/options/options.css` remains 61 physical lines / 50 non-empty lines by `Get-Content`.
- Retired tokens/surfaces: Dashboard panel-local native `click`/`focusin` listener installation and cleanup in `DashboardPanel.tsx`.

### 2026-06-20 Dashboard React Keydown Boundary

- Re-audited `DashboardPanel.tsx`, `DashboardView.tsx`, `dashboard-controller.ts`, `options-controller.ts`, `InlineMenu`, `Popover`, current options event-listener residuals, and the previously named `dashboard-drag-preview` candidate before editing. - Replaced the visible Dashboard panel's persistent `document.addEventListener('keydown', handleDashboardDocumentKeyDown, true)` registration/removal with a React-owned `onKeyDownCapture` handler on the Dashboard section. The controller handler was renamed to `handleDashboardPanelKeyDown` to reflect the new event boundary.
- Added programmatic focus to the Dashboard section on entry with `tabIndex={-1}` and `focus({ preventScroll: true })` when focus is not already inside the panel. This preserves the previous "Escape exits Dashboard even before focusing a child control" behavior without keeping a document listener alive.
- Preserved Escape semantics through the existing controller logic: Base UI card menu Escape closes the menu without exiting Dashboard, tag editor Escape closes the editor, active drag Escape cancels the overlay, and a later Dashboard-section Escape exits the Dashboard route. The retained typed drag action path continues to own drag pointer continuation/cancellation.
- Existing Base UI composition was left unchanged; this slice changed only the Dashboard keydown event ownership and panel focus boundary.
- No stylesheet lines were changed in this slice; current `src/options/options.css` remains 61 physical lines / 50 non-empty lines by `Get-Content`.
- Retired tokens/surfaces: `handleDashboardDocumentKeyDown`, the visible Dashboard panel document `keydown` listener installation/removal, and the remaining Dashboard-owned source-level `document.addEventListener(...)` path.

### 2026-06-20 Newtab Reduced-Motion Selector Retirement

- Re-audited the current newtab reduced-motion selectors before editing. The remaining `src/newtab/newtab.css` reduced-motion block only coordinated app-level shell descendants, settings trigger surfaces, bookmark tiles, and Speed Dial cards; no controller behavior, ARIA relationship, drag/drop path, or storage state depended on the CSS selector names.
- Added React-owned reduced-motion class constants in `NewtabApp.tsx` for the newtab shell descendant/pseudo-element guard and settings trigger zone/button surfaces. The settings/dashboard trigger buttons also carry the important self reduced-motion utility because the previous non-important `motion-reduce:transition-none` did not win over the arbitrary transition declaration in the generated cascade.
- Added React-owned reduced-motion class constants to `bookmarkTileClasses.ts` and `speedDialClasses.ts`, preserving the stable `.bookmark-tile` and `.newtab-speed-dial-card` marker classes while moving duration/transform behavior into Tailwind utilities.
- Removed the trailing `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block from `src/newtab/newtab.css`, including `.newtab-shell :where(*, *::before, *::after)`, `.settings-trigger-zone`, `.settings-trigger`, `.bookmark-tile`, and `.newtab-speed-dial-card` selector branches.
- `src/newtab/newtab.css` is now 192 physical lines / 171 non-empty lines by `Get-Content`, down from 204 physical / 182 non-empty. This slice retired 12 physical CSS lines, 11 non-empty CSS lines, the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` newtab selector block, and the old shell/settings/tile/card reduced-motion selector group.

### 2026-06-20 Newtab Background Video Loading Selector Retirement

- Re-audited the remaining `.newtab-app.loading-wallpaper .newtab-background-video` selector before editing. `newtab-controller.ts` owns video readiness through `waitForBackgroundVideoSourceReady()` and `dispatchNewtabBackgroundMediaView()`, while wallpaper loading/ready state is already published through `dispatchNewtabInstantWallpaperView()` and consumed by React. No controller behavior, ARIA relationship, storage mutation path, or event listener reads the selector.
- Moved video loading opacity into `NewtabBackgroundLayer.tsx` with React-owned Tailwind classes. The background layer now separates the shared fixed media shell from image/video opacity classes, keeps `.newtab-background-video` as a stable marker, and receives `loadingWallpaper` from `NewtabShell` so the video remains hidden until the same parent render removes the `.loading-wallpaper` app state.

### 2026-06-21 Newtab Dashboard Overlay Nodes Bridge

- Re-audited the dashboard overlay node-change bridge before editing. The old bridge only transported React-published overlay/trigger/frame node changes from `newtab-dashboard-overlay-store.ts` through a custom `window` event, `NewtabApp`, and `newtab-window-store` back into the controller; the controller handler only schedules overlay positioning/focus work and does not depend on the event object.
- Replaced that app-wide custom event bridge with a store-level subscription. `newtab-dashboard-overlay-store.ts` now owns `nodesListeners`, `subscribeNewtabDashboardOverlayNodes(listener)`, and microtask-delayed node-change emission from `setNewtabDashboardOverlayNodes()`, preserving the previous "after refs are published" timing without dispatching `CustomEvent` on `window`.

### 2026-06-21 Dashboard Drag Overlay Hidden Token Retirement

- Re-read `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` before editing. This slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; the dashboard drag overlay is a React-rendered dashboard layer with pointer events and state-driven visibility.

The drag overlay state has separate `visible` and `closing` flags; hiding directly with `hidden={!state.visible}` would cut off the existing close fade-out.
- `DashboardPanel.tsx` now removes the rendered `state.visible ? '' : '!hidden'` class branch from the dashboard drag overlay root and uses native `hidden={!state.visible && !state.closing}` instead. This keeps the overlay unhidden while `state.closing` is true so the existing `DASHBOARD_DRAG_OVERLAY_CLOSING_STATE_CLASS` fade-out can still run, then returns the layer to native hidden after `clearDashboardDragOverlayContent()` sets `visible=false` and `closing=false`.

### 2026-06-21 Dashboard Tag Editor Hidden Token Retirement

- Re-read `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/popover` before editing because the dashboard tag editor composes the shared Base UI Popover wrapper. The tag editor state has separate `visible` and `closing` flags; `closeDashboardTagEditor()` publishes `closing=true` while `visible` remains true, then clears the editor after the 220ms close delay. This keeps the editor root unhidden while `state.closing` is true so the existing `DASHBOARD_TAG_EDITOR_ROOT_CLOSING_STATE_CLASS` and `DASHBOARD_TAG_EDITOR_PANEL_CLOSING_STATE_CLASS` exit state can run, then returns the layer to native hidden after the controller clears `visible` and `closing`.

### 2026-06-21 Popup Clear Search Native Hidden Cleanup

- Re-read `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and `https://base-ui.com/react/components/button.md` before editing because the clear-search control uses the shared Base UI `Button` wrapper. The slice kept the existing Button composition, explicit `type="button"`, `unstyled` styling path, accessible label, `#clear-search` id, clear-search dispatch, and search-input focus return.
- Re-audited `PopupChromeHost.tsx`, `src/ui/base/Button.tsx`, `popup-controller.ts`, `popup-controller-store.ts`, current built popup assets, current popup CSS, and prior clear-search inventory notes before editing. Clear-search visibility is driven by `getPopupChromeViewModel().search.clearVisible` from `state.searchQuery`, and clicking the button dispatches the typed `clear-search` action to `setSearchQuery('', { immediate: true })` plus `focusSearchInput()`. No CSS selector, controller query, ARIA relationship, bookmark mutation path, storage schema, permission path, tab path, or Tailwind ancestor selector depends on `#clear-search` carrying a rendered Tailwind `hidden` utility.
- `PopupChromeHost.tsx` now renders the clear-search Button with `className={clearSearchButtonClass}` and native `hidden={!state.search.clearVisible}` instead of composing `state.search.clearVisible ? '' : 'hidden'` into the rendered class list.
- Preserved the stable `#search-input`, `#clear-search`, and `#natural-search-toggle` ids, Base UI Button native button semantics, explicit `type="button"`, `aria-label="清空搜索"`, search query dispatch, clear-search toast notice, and focus return to `#search-input`.
- This slice did not touch any stylesheet rule. CSS line counts after the current build remain: `src/options/options.css` 5 physical / 5 non-empty, `src/popup/popup.css` 133 / 116, `src/newtab/newtab.css` 125 / 108, `src/shared/ui-refresh.css` 85 / 76, `src/styles/globals.css` 100 / 83, `src/styles/tailwind.css` 18 / 16, and `src/styles/tokens.css` 23 / 23.

The static `hero-eyebrow hidden` utility and input pseudo-element `:hidden` utilities in `PopupChromeHost.tsx` remain separate documented residuals and were not changed in this slice.

### 2026-06-21 Newtab Bookmark Icon Native Hidden Cleanup

- Re-read `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` before editing. This slice did not touch a specific Base UI primitive API; it changed only the React-rendered bookmark icon/fallback shell used by newtab bookmark tiles, Speed Dial cards, and drag ghosts.
- Re-audited the current worktree state, migration design, this inventory, `BookmarkIconShell.tsx`, `bookmarkTileClasses.ts`, `NewtabBookmarkContent.tsx`, `NewtabSpeedDialPanel.tsx`, `NewtabDragLayerHost.tsx`, `speedDialClasses.ts`, `favicon-cache.ts`, `newtab-controller.ts`, current newtab CSS, and current built newtab assets before validating. Bookmark favicon display is driven by React state inside `BookmarkIconShell` after `img.onerror`; no controller query, storage schema, bookmark mutation path, permission path, tab path, ARIA relationship, or focus-return path depends on the favicon/fallback children carrying rendered Tailwind `hidden` utilities or ancestor arbitrary selectors.
- `BookmarkIconShell.tsx` now keeps the stable `.bookmark-icon-shell`, `.bookmark-favicon`, `.bookmark-fallback`, `.custom-icon`, and `.favicon-missing` markers while moving missing-favicon visibility to native `hidden={missing}` on the favicon image and native `hidden={!missing}` on the fallback span.
- The fallback span now receives the visible-state `grid` utility only when the favicon is missing; the favicon image receives `block` only when visible. This avoids a rendered `hidden` class and avoids overriding native `[hidden]` display semantics.
- Preserved the existing favicon URL generation path through `chrome.runtime.getURL('/_favicon/')`, fallback label derivation, custom icon sizing, Speed Dial mark class override, drag ghost icon refs, `aria-hidden="true"` shell semantics, lazy/eager fetch attributes, and missing-favicon `.favicon-missing` marker.
- This slice did not touch any stylesheet rule. CSS line counts after the current build remain: `src/options/options.css` 5 physical / 5 non-empty, `src/popup/popup.css` 133 / 116, `src/newtab/newtab.css` 125 / 108, `src/shared/ui-refresh.css` 85 / 76, `src/styles/globals.css` 100 / 83, `src/styles/tailwind.css` 18 / 16, and `src/styles/tokens.css` 23 / 23.
- Retired tokens/dependencies: bookmark icon fallback hiding via `bookmark-fallback absolute hidden`, favicon/fallback switching via `favicon-missing [&_.bookmark-fallback]:grid [&_.bookmark-favicon]:hidden`, and the rendered `bookmark-favicon ... block` utility when the favicon is natively hidden.
- `npm run typecheck` passed.
- `npm run build` passed; the only noted build warning remains the known Vite mixed dynamic/static import warning for `src/popup/search-lookup.ts`.

- The smoke seeded two bookmark-bar bookmarks, fulfilled one generated `/_favicon/?pageUrl=...` request with a valid PNG, and fulfilled the other with invalid image bytes to trigger the production `img.onerror` fallback path without creating unrelated browser console errors.
- The smoke confirmed the loaded favicon tile renders `.bookmark-favicon` with `hidden=false`, computed `display: block`, no rendered `hidden` class token, and a fallback `.bookmark-fallback` with native `hidden=true` / computed `display: none` and no rendered `hidden` class token.
- The smoke confirmed the missing-favicon tile keeps the `.favicon-missing` shell marker, renders the image with native `hidden=true`, computed `display: none`, no rendered `hidden` class token, and renders the fallback label `M` with `hidden=false`, computed `display: grid`, and no rendered `hidden` class token.
- The same smoke confirmed generated favicon URLs still include `/_favicon/` and `pageUrl=`, zero bookmark create/move/update/remove/removeTree calls, zero permission requests, zero tab create/update calls, zero storage writes, zero `data-react-island-host` nodes, and no page errors, actionable console errors, request failures, or HTTP error responses. The first smoke attempt failed only because the temporary harness loaded production HTML through `file://`, which blocked module/CSS assets by browser CORS policy; the harness was changed to a local HTTP static server. A later attempt failed only because the harness used a 404 favicon response that the browser logged; the harness was changed to valid status plus invalid image bytes. ## Smoke Paths To Preserve

Runnable verification entries now exist in the repository `scripts/` directory:

- `npm test` runs `scripts/run-tests.mjs`, which executes `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build`.
- `npm run smoke:extension` runs `scripts/smoke-extension.mjs`, which launches a real unpacked extension from `dist` in a disposable Playwright-managed Chromium profile.

Future manual or Playwright smoke paths should preserve at least the following coverage:

- Popup:
  - load popup
  - search bookmarks
  - open folder
  - edit bookmark
  - move bookmark
  - delete bookmark through confirmation
  - smart save prompt path
  - AI not-configured path
- Newtab:
  - load newtab
  - display configured bookmarks
  - search bar query and clear
  - settings drawer open/close
  - background settings preview and commit
  - folder and speed dial interactions
  - dashboard overlay open/close
  - drag reorder or drag move path
- Options/dashboard:
  - route navigation
  - availability settings and dry interaction
  - duplicate selection workflow
  - recycle restore/delete workflow
  - backup export/import preview path
  - AI settings model selection path
  - dashboard search/filter/select/card actions, including Base UI card menu and tag editor
  - embedded dashboard mode from newtab

## Final Status

The React runtime migration goal is closed against the current design Cleanup Criteria and this inventory. No further compatibility-removal slice is required for this goal.

Retained residuals and reasons:

- `public/instant-wallpaper-boot.js` and `NewtabInstantWallpaperHost.tsx` retain first-paint wallpaper bootstrap and cleanup behavior only.
- React root `document.getElementById` lookups remain in the three entry points because React still needs a mount node.
- Media/canvas, clipboard, and download helpers retain direct element creation for browser APIs rather than UI rendering.
- Small CSS files remain for root sizing, page background, shared token, Tailwind entry, global transition, and first-paint/media scaffolding.
- Generated Tailwind `.hidden` output remains in `dist/assets/valueToPercent-DbwA9elT.css` because the app still intentionally uses Tailwind hidden variants and native-hidden selectors.
- `src/ui/base` remains the shared Base UI/Tailwind wrapper layer; the old handcrafted `src/ui/primitives` compatibility directory is deleted.

Before any future slice that touches Base UI primitives, re-read `https://base-ui.com/llms.txt` and the relevant primitive docs. Future smoke work should preserve the popup, newtab, options/dashboard, and embedded dashboard coverage listed above.
